Sarring Trevor Eve, with Paul McGann

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: General Wellesley receives unwanted reinforcements from the King of Spain in the form of the Royal Irish Company and Sharpe is charged with transforming them into an effective fighting unit. When Kiely learns his wife is pregnant, he sends her away out of harm's way, but she is captured. With Sean Bean, Daragh O'Malley and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Ally's dodgy dealings take a turn for the worse, and Barry has difficulty controlling Hazel who seems to be suffering from an overdose of sun, sea and sangria - to say nothing of gambling fever. Acclaimed comedy drama, starring Bill Paterson, Timothy Spall and Melanie Hill

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Barry finally announces his long-awaited wedding to Hazel is definitely going ahead and Oz secretly embarks on a romance of his own. Needless to say, Ally is concocting another dodgy scheme in the background and, as he puts it into operation, the lads sail off over the horizon. Comedy drama, starring Jimmy Nail, Timothy Spall and Tim Healy

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are hired to create a garden as a wedding present, and find a baby under a gooseberry bush. The discovery brings out Rosemary's maternal side as the pair investigate who abandoned the child. Their task is complicated by the murder of an artistic neighbour - and the two mysterious incidents appear to be connected. Starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo visit a resort in Spain to create a trophy garden for a pro-am tennis tournament run by a former champion. When one of the competitors is found dead, everyone assumes it to be a gruesome accident - but there is more to the case than meets the eye. While investigating, Laura enters the mysterious world of bullfighting, and Rosemary rediscovers her love of motor scooters

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The green-fingered amateur detectives work on a garden for an open-air theatre, where Rosemary's cousin is due to perform. However, the production suffers a setback when an actor shoots himself during rehearsal - and the gun is found to have contained live bullets instead of blanks. A member of the cast gets an attack of stage fright, and Laura is obliged to take her place in the play as the duo try to find the culprit. Drama, with Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: The owner and curator of Brokenwood's Historic Village, Charity Highmore-Browne, dies after being struck by an arrow. Mike and his team delve into the realm of New Zealand's colonial past as they deal with suspects including a blacksmith, a missionary, the local postmaster and the victim's sister. Neill Rea stars

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A murder is committed at the local army barracks using a legendary broken sword, and the incident leads the priest to investigate another death at Dunkirk 13 years previously. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: DI Max Harper believes he has enough evidence to connect the murders of Rosie Turner and Daniel Pembrook. Chief Inspector Briggs is shocked to discover the extent of Sergeant Fenton's deception, and even though he reveals Lenny Powell's connection to the brothel, it does not appear to be enough to save him. Starring Ben Turner

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A Scottish writer who has made a name for himself in Hollywood receives a death threat, propelling the grumpy Glaswegian detective into a murky world of jealousy and deceit. Mark McManus stars in the mystery, with James Macpherson, Blythe Duff, Ian Anders and John McGlynn

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: Four-part adaptation of Charlotte Bronte's novel about an orphan girl who struggles to survive the harsh treatment of a boarding school to embark on a career as a governess. Jane takes up her first position at the home of the alluring and unpredictable Edward Rochester, under the watchful eye of laundrywoman Grace Poole. Ruth Wilson, Toby Stephens and Francesca Annis star

programme.title: The Last Post
programme.desc: Drama about military police and their families faced with political and personal conflicts in a 1960s British colony on the Red Sea coast. An officer and his wife arrive in Aden, where he is to replace a much-loved captain. He soon has to adjust to life on the frontline, along with resentment among the men that he was appointed instead of the already-present Lt Ed Laithwaite. Honor is taken under the wing of Laithwaite's wife, Alison, who had been having an affair with Captain Page. Starring Jessie Buckley and Jeremy Neumark Jones

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Luca is backed into a corner when his past comes back to haunt him, Bear and Sid go on a double date, and Karen meets a mother who is desperate to save her sick daughter

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu are hired to locate a photograph stolen from a local pub, whose owner is in the middle of a feud with another local family - only for events to take a deadly turn

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Steve Brown takes a couple round the West Yorkshire market town of Wetherby, showing them properties for their £550,000 budget and also exploring the local cafes and horticulture

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Engaged couple Nick and Donnie compete with the help of chefs Jeremy Pang and Anna Haugh, seeing who can come up with the best meal against the clock. Hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Sellers try to get a deal for a painting by early 20th-century artist William Russell Flint, a tin rocket toy, a set of retro formica furniture, and a carved Japanese walking stick

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest, presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat feels uneasy when Phil suggests that next time they should meet up in a hotel, worried that things are getting a little too serious for her liking. Lily confronts Ruby about the miscarriage, telling her it was not Stacey's fault. Denise suggests asking Jack for help with the family's problems - but Chelsea does not like the thought of him sniffing around - and Keegan is not happy to hear about Tiffany and Dotty's big money-making plan

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents the final of the contest, which sees the three remaining participants aiming to become top dog-groomer - and win themselves the Golden Stanley trophy to prove it. All they have to do is execute one of the toughest challenges in the industry - a continental clip on an elegant standard poodle. As if that were not enough, they have to guess a breed of dog while blindfolded, before one last dogwalk parade. Who will impress judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor the most?

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Contestant pairs Latia and Rose, and Chris and Luke answer a series of questions to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board - and the more bars they build up, the more they can win to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the higher they go, the greater the chance of falling, requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of guests to answer questions from a virtual audience. Her panellists this week are Secretary of State for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy Kwasi Kwarteng MP, Shadow Foreign Secretary Lisa Nandy MP, founder and CEO of Palmer Automative Andy Palmer, economist and Chief Executive of the New Economics Forum Miatta Fahnbulleh and businessman and entrepreneur Theo Paphitis

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring all the goals, analysis of the talking points and post-match reaction from managers and players. All of the day's games will be covered, with the panel of former professionals analysing the tactics employed by the teams, as well as highlighting any deficiencies that led to a team's downfall

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin follows progress on a mother-of-two's dreams of transforming a Victorian house in Newcastle-under-Lyme, Staffordshire, into a wonderful family home. Lucy Alexander meets a film extra modernising an investment flat in Thamesmead, south-east London, and Martin Roberts catches up on a four-year project to upgrade a family home in East Taphouse in Cornwall

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste report on a woman suffering from what she felt was a type of Stockholm syndrome, in that she was unable to escape a relationship with a man who took tens of thousands of pounds from her

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: National Crime Agency officers seize cash and drugs from a suspected County Lines drug dealer, while Border Force officers at Gatwick sell off a huge haul of gold seized from a passenger. Presented by Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, where experts Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper help today's reds and blue teams, before heading to auction where Charles Hanson is set to wield the gavel. Kate also has some top tips when it comes to jewellery, revealing how to spot an imitation from the real thing.

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu delve into the ultra-competitive world of private tutoring, while a reunion with an old Rada friend, presents Sebastian with a choice about his future that could change everything for the agency. Tamzin Outhwaite guest stars, with Mark Benton and Jo Joyner

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Korean food is set to be the next big thing in the UK, so bikers Dave Myers and Si King conclude their culinary journey around Asia by finding out more about it. Out with some young Seoulites, they discover the Korean barbecue and cook for a Korean pop star. After a lesson in making kimchi, the bikers head south for a local squid festival.

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman visits the seaside town of Ayr, combing through its good-looking property stock and popular culture haunts. With £400,000 in their pocket, her buyers have pinpointed Ayr as a potential perfect town because it puts them a walk from the beach and a train ride from the buzz of Glasgow. They soon discover it's a real gem of a place, with a multi-generational crowd thanks to its university campus and lively theatre scene, as well as award-winning local seafood on the waterfront

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Romantic novelists Mandy and Rachel do battle in the kitchen, with the help of chefs Akis Petretzikis and Ellis Barrie. The duo are tasked to cook against the clock, producing dishes based on a budget of £5, from tomato carpaccio with baked feta to halloumi saganaki. However, they need to be on their mettle when the 10-Minute Challenge throws up tinned ham and treacle. Hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Dealers Adi Higham, James Gooch, James Broad, Lucy Ryder Richardson and Tash Francis compete to buy an ornate clock by the distinguished British horologist Charles Frodsham, an antique tombola machine, a 19th-century armchair and a vintage coffee set. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Martin Clunes round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Tommy Cooper at the BBC
programme.desc: Marking the centenary of his birth, Lenny Henry presents a selection of performances by the comedian. This collection features some of Cooper's most famous routines, and reveals that his reputation for terrible tricks that inevitably went wrong was all based on an act, as his on-stage bumbling persona hid a real talent as a magician

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The first quarter-final sees the four talented heat-winners return to fight for a place in knockout week in the Critic's Test. Their brief - set by Grace Dent, one of the country's toughest food critics - is to cook their take on a great British pudding. Presented by Gregg Wallace and John Torode

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea is horrified to learn Denise told Jack everything and is desperate to find him before he reports them. Whitney and Kush decide to go public with their relationship, and while Kat takes the news surprisingly well, Gray is clearly hiding his displeasure. Wise words from Mitch inspire Mick to make a decision, Ben encourages Phil to try online dating to find someone more suitable than Kat, and Dotty and Keegan are at loggerheads over Tiffany working as a hostess

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Under the eye of the acerbic chef, contestant pairs Chris and Luke, and Sian and Jacob answer a series of questions in order to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board - the more bars they build up, the more they can win up to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the more bars that accumulate, the greater the chance they will fall down requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve to triumph in a game show that's as unpredictable in nature as its host

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Line of Duty stars Vicky McClure and Adrian Dunbar talk about the hotly awaited sixth series of the BBC's crime drama. Star Wars' Daisy Ridley chats about starring in sci-fi adventure Chaos Walking, Breaking Bad's Bryan Cranston reflects on his role as a New Orleans judge in the TV drama Your Honor and Brit star Chiwetel Ejiofor promotes his romantic comedy heist, Locked Down. Plus, Imelda May performs Made to Love

programme.title: PRU
programme.desc: Halil, Hanna, Jaeden and Belle are four very different 15-year-olds who have one thing in common - they have all been permanently excluded from mainstream education and find themselves having to go through Year 10 together at their local Pupil Referral Unit. There, they are tutored by their long-suffering behaviour manager. One-off comedy, with Umit Ulgen, Kosar Ali, Michael Boahen, Jaye Eservas and Kerry Godliman

programme.title: Source Code (2011)
programme.desc: A military pilot is the subject of a top-secret experiment that transfers his mind into the body of a man killed in a terrorist bombing on a train, but only for the last eight minutes of the dead man's life. His mission is to use that time to find out who is responsible and if they intend to strike again. Sci-fi thriller, starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Michelle Monaghan, Vera Farmiga and Jeffrey Wright

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Gennaro Contaldo and Anna Jones, and guest Stacey Solomon. Drinks expert Helen McGinn joins via video link to pick wine to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Recipes That Made Me
programme.desc: Nisha Katona travels around the UK meeting passionate home cooks to discover how family recipes are passed down through generations from across the Indian Subcontinent. In the first episode, Nisha travels to the West Midlands to discover authentic family recipes from the Punjab, and learns about their love of bread, and makes tandoori chicken and spinach paneer

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents a look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Manchester City v Manchester United and Chelsea v Everton

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and David Harper help the red and blue teams find bargains in the antique shops of Dorking in Surrey, while host Anita Manning visits a Roman villa in West Sussex

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey, including a retro two-seater sofa and a vintage sewing box

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a spiritual businesswoman and her best friend to find a calming Hampshire home, and experiences the heights of Portsmouth's Spinnaker Tower

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal returns with the culinary duel in which two chefs create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought on to the show by the contestants assisting each of them. In the first edition, Jeremy Pang and Akis Petretzikis are joined by brothers Justin and Adrian

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers Joe Pasquale, Grace Dent, Mwaksy and Robin Ince to take to the black chair and play for charity to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions. The contestants' chosen subjects are the Planet of the Apes movies, Edward VI, The Gospel of Mark, and The Young Ones

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a new family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees two twin brothers from Leeds answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes, which they plan to use to further their education and provide some help for their mother. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as past and present cast members of Casualty take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Di Botcher, George Rainsford, Ian Bleasdale, Sue Devaney, Azuka Oforka, James Redmond, Clive Mantle and Julia Watson

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jan receives a call demanding she steal drugs from the hospital and smuggle them into prison. When a patient she knows dies, her sadness is tempered by the opportunity the situation presents to obtain the drugs she needs. Connie lashes out in her frustration over Jacob's new relationship, and a friendly bet between Rosa and Charlie soon gets out of control

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Denton fights for her life as disturbing details emerge of a conspiracy involving corrupt officers, and Arnott uncovers startling new details about the detective's personal life which appear to support her claim that she is being framed. Meanwhile, Hastings puts his career on the line to pursue a high-level target. Crime thriller, starring Adrian Dunbar and Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Aston Villa v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Villa Park and Brighton & Hove Albion v Leicester City at Amex Stadium. The Villans were looking to do the double over their Midlands rivals after prevailing 1-0 at Molineux in December, while Leicester were hoping to boost their top four hopes with a third win of the season over the Seagulls, having defeated them at home in the league and FA Cup. Plus, Burnley v Arsenal and Sheffield United v Southampton

programme.title: Margin Call (2011)
programme.desc: A bitter executive is dismissed from an investment bank, but plans his revenge by handing secret information on the company's fortunes to a junior analyst. When the information is decoded, it reveals that the company is on the brink of a devastating financial crisis, a revelation that is soon spreading throughout the workforce. Drama, starring Kevin Spacey, Paul Bettany and Jeremy Irons

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: A woman who was drawn into a psychologically abusive relationship and conned into handing over more than £60,000 of her life savings

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: The return of the property relocation advice programme, with Jonnie Irwin heading to Stratford-upon-Avon in Warwickshire with an adventurous and active couple who have a budget of £650,000

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: In the semi-final, the last four competitors face a breed that judge Colin Taylor is world-famous for grooming - the lamb-like Bedlington terrier. In Dogology, crossbreed puppies enter the salon and the groomers must identify the breeds of their two parents. One of the most dramatic transformations takes place on the Dogwalk when the dogs take the shape of completely different animals, to the delight of their owners. Presented by Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright is joined by Olympic gymnast Max Whitlock for some flexibility sessions

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Thinking Thursday, including maths, drama and the Big Read

programme.title: Bang Goes the Theory
programme.desc: The team investigates the reality of dangerous strains of influenza such as H1N1, or swine flu, which caused widespread panic in late 2009. Liz Bonnin witnesses the making of the vaccines that offer protection during the winter and Maggie Philbin visits a laboratory where healthy volunteers are being deliberately infected to learn how the body is attacked. Jem Stansfield explains flu on a cellular level, showing how it can mutate from the cause of winter snuffles to a disease capable of killing millions

programme.title: The Nine to Five with Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Stacey takes the work experience teenagers to Sunderland to experience life at one of the country's biggest contact centres. Elliot, Mersadi, Skye, Abbey and Hakeem join the 650 customer experience representatives taking calls on car, home and life insurance

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of old-school recipes. They include Keith Floyd's Irish stew, the Hairy Bikers' meatloaf, James Martin's ultimate bacon butty, a treacle tart from Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood, and a chili con carne from Lorraine Pascale

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Eric Knowles goes head to head with Ochuko Ojiri in Villeneuve-lès-Avignon, France, with Ochuko on the hunt for retro chic, while Eric sniffs out classic French antiques

programme.title: The Great Rift: Africa's Wild Heart
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the grasslands of east Africa, and a line of volcanos stretching from Ethiopia to Tanzania, whose peaks provide a refuge for many wildlife species - which use surprising strategies to survive in such a challenging environment

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Catherine Southon and David Fletcher scour a crowd at Ushaw in Co Durham to find objects to take off to auction. Catherine uncovers a large collection of glassware and stoneware dug up in the north east of England, while David finds an American money box in the shape of a bulldog. Host Paul Martin visits the Oriental Museum at Durham University to find out about the collection of Malcolm McDonald, a British diplomat who was passionate about Asian ceramics

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nihal Arthanayake, Jake Humphrey, Patsy Kensit and Mae Martin test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team are in Bradford-on-Avon to help single mother Fiona transform her 110-year-old narrowboat into a thriving short-rental business. Faced with an almost empty shell, it looks like being a big job. Before long, Zoe is painting and renovating the interior, while Simon and Ian maximise the space with foldout beds and a copper-fronted bath in the stern

programme.title: Harry Potter: A History of Magic
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the myths, traditions and everyday artefacts that inspired JK Rowling as she crafted the wizarding world depicted in her celebrated Harry Potter series. The British Library is marking the 20th anniversary of the publication of Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone with an exhibition showcasing unseen manuscripts, drawings, drafts and personal items from Rowling's private archives, and this programme sheds light on how the objects on display helped to fire the author's imagination over the course of the past two decades. Famous fans offer playful readings that recreate some of the best-loved spells, potion recipes and magical moments from the books, and the programme introduces individuals who have made the magic of the novels a reality, from real-life wand-makers to passionate Quidditch enthusiasts. Narrated by Imelda Staunton

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Designers Manoj Malde and Tom Massey come up with alternative ideas for the West Sussex garden of wheelchair user Tracey, who can only venture into certain parts of her current space with assistance, and her husband Jake, who dreams of having a low-maintenance plot that he doesn't have to keep looking neat and tidy. Plus, Joel Bird helps a Liverpool couple turn their tiny, dull courtyard into a colourful paradise. Presented by Angela Scanlon

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti work in Ashford Castle, Co Mayo. Set in 350 acres, it was founded 800 years ago by the Anglo-Norman De Burgo family, and much later became the family home of the Guinness family. With the help of hotel historian, Fintan, Giles discovers that the castle has had a rollercoaster journey through the centuries, sporadically reaching moments of real jeopardy, only to be saved by a turn of events or new owners. Giles and Monica soon learn that the secret of the castle's success is the people who work here and the hotel's strong, long-running community ties

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A look back at the year when Simply Red, the Housemartins, Sinitta, the Communards and Swing Out Sister hit the charts. It was a time that saw the advent of house music, the big bang in the money markets and political fatigue on the left. How did these real-world events make their mark on the nation's most popular show?

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Being Bridget Jones
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating 25 years of Helen Fielding's hapless creation. The programme examines the ways in which society's attitudes to women have shifted in 25 years, along with the ways in which female writers have told their own stories since Bridget made her debut. Featuring interviews with Helen Fielding, and the friends she based the original characters on, as well as tributes from celebrity fans

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: When a 15-year-old teenager is fatally stabbed in a park in Coventry, the forensics team must identify the murderers through evidence left behind at the crime scene. However, the weapon is nowhere to be found, so they are reliant on just two items - a mobile phone found a short distance from the body and the victim's blood-soaked jacket

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The competition reaches the halfway point, where the contestants are challenged by Mary Berry to prepare the ultimate Sunday roast. The only rule is that the meal must involve roasted meat and some form of potatoes - they are otherwise free to do whatever they want. Two cooks compete to produce the best pork crackling, while another adds a Morrocan twist. For the second task, Chris Bavin challenges the cooks to make banana bread without using flour, while the final round involves a classic French dessert

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: In the quarter-final, the remaining celebrities show off their celebration cakes for Mary Berry's Ultimate Challenge. They also take part in Chris Bavin's all-vegetarian Rustle Up, tasked with making the humble chickpea the star of their dishes, in a bid to avoid Angela Hartnett's dreaded Eliminator. Presented by Claudia Winkleman

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste meet a woman whose online friend tried to gain her trust and affection to fleece her out of thousands of pounds

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a three-bed terrace in Cumbria and a former commercial property in Stoke-on-Trent where the new owners have a very specific plan. Martin Roberts is on the south coast in Southampton to see a two-bed terrace

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Helle and David travel from Wiltshire with a diamond and sapphire engagement ring passed down three generations of marriages, seeking the help of goldsmith Richard Talman. Next up, Luthier Julyan Wallis restores Jimmy Spencer's Hofner electric guitar, before metal expert Dominic Chinea works on a 200-year-old church weathervane. Finally, leather expert Susie Fletcher turns her attention to an old fly-fishing bait wallet

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: TV presenter Radzi Chinyanganya joins Mark Wright to participate in his daily dose of workout sessions

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the morning session on day one of the biennial event, held this year at Arena Torun in Poland

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Feelgood Friday, featuring some well-known faces

programme.title: Bang Goes the Theory
programme.desc: The team tries to get to the bottom of the recent flooding, asking whether global warming played a part or if it was simply the extreme end of the natural cycle. Maggie Philbin investigates the real threat of storm surges as water levels rise, Liz Bonnin tries to find natural ways to slow the flow and the duo are joined by former Ground Force gardener Charlie Dimmock, who contemplates whether the public's growing fondness for patios and decking may have played a part in creating urban flash floods

programme.title: The Nine to Five with Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley takes five 16-18 year-olds into different UK industries. In this episode, they go to a high-end trainers factory near Carlisle to experience the manufacturing industry. As each is set to work on key stages of the manufacturing process, it's not long before things start to fall apart. Back at their house in the countryside, things boil over when some members of the group yet again have to bail out those who cannot pay their way

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Eric Knowles faces Chuko Ojiri at an auction in Colchester, where Eric tries his luck with a Victorian Whatnot, and Chucko attempts to dazzle with an expensive jewellery project

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and Chuko Ojiri search a car-boot sale in Chesterfield, where items of interest include an antique coin scales and a John Wayne mirror

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary following a research project at Edinburgh Zoo aimed at providing chimpanzees with the training necessary to shoot a film. Chimp specialist Betsy Herrelko tries to communicate with the primates using video technology, as they are given cameras and taught how to use them. The filmed results can be seen at the end of the programme. Stuart McQuarrie narrates

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Caroline Hawley and Nick Hall are on hand at the City Hall in Hull, hoping to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Caroline is delighted to find a painting by local artist Steven Dews and Nick is bedazzled by some iconic 1970s jewellery. Presenter Paul Martin visits the Wilberforce House Museum in Hull's Old Town to hear the story of Hull-born William Wilberforce, the great anti-slave trade campaigner

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nihal Arthanayake, Jake Humphrey, Patsy Kensit and Mae Martin go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games for the final time, prior to the week's overall winner being declared

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the evening session on day one of the biennial event, held this year at Arena Torun in Poland. The first medals of the championships are awarded in the women's shot put, 3,000m and pentathlon, and men's long jump, shot put and 1500m, the last of which sees home favourite Marcin Lewandowski going for a third successive gold

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: The cultural historian heads to the vast Konya Plain of Turkey, tracking a young explorer who found the world's oldest city and rewrote the beginning of civilisation - until his reputation was destroyed by scandal

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools welcomes global superstar Tom Jones into his studio to discuss his musical journey, loves and influences, illustrated by classic clips from the show. He and Jools also perform a track from his forthcoming 41st studio album Surrounded by Time, which reimagines a diverse set of songs of personal importance to him throughout his illustrious career. Plus, a performance from the restored Alexandra Palace Theatre in north London by Mercury Prize-winning genre-bending rockers Wolf Alice. Pa Salieu, the winner of the BBC Sound of 2021, arguably the UKs hottest new rapper, also performs

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark introduces analysis of the day's national and international events

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Former president Gaius Baltar faces an uncertain future while imprisoned on charges of treason. Apollo visits the new drinking establishment with Tyrol and confides that his marriage is failing. Sci-fi drama, starring Jamie Bamber, James Callis and Aaron Douglas

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Helo suspects a doctor may be murdering his patients when racial tensions flare up aboard the Galactica. Meanwhile, there is increasing agitation among the fleet at news of Baltar's forthcoming trial. Sci-fi drama starring Edward James Olmos

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: The fleet continues to be relieved at the lack of Cylon contact, but Tyrol and Cally are put at risk when they become trapped in a malfunctioning airlock. Meanwhile, Adama remembers his late wife Carolanne on the day of their would-be wedding anniversary

programme.title: Sailing
programme.desc: Live coverage of day one from Auckland, as defending champions Emirates Team New Zealand take on the winner of the PRADA Cup

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Ninjas, sumo, a life-size origami boat and costumes straight out of a manga comic are dreamt up by Japan-loving brother and sister Sunee and Taeya - and it's a race against time as they wait for a very special delivery to arrive all the way from Tokyo

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: A nine-year-old from Brentwood seeks a marble-run den in which he can pursue his ultimate dream of becoming a rollercoaster designer

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The winners of the 2021 Blue Peter Book Awards are announced

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents the final of the contestant, which sees the three remaining contestants aiming to become top dog-groomer - and win themselves the Golden Stanley trophy to prove it. All they have to do is execute one of the toughest challenges in the industry - a continental clip on an elegant standard poodle. As if that were not enough, they have to guess a breed of dog while blindfolded, before one last dogwalk parade. Who will impress judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor the most?

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the morning session on day two of the biennial event, held this year at Arena Torun in Poland. With commentary from Steve Cram, Steve Backley and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms revisits archive interviews with some of Britain's biggest stars of the 1960s, including Sean Connery, George Lazenby, Peter Sellers, Peter O'Toole and Julie Christie

programme.title: The Ipcress File (1965)
programme.desc: An intelligence agent investigating the kidnap and brainwashing of Britain's most renowned scientists unpicks a complex chain of connections and high-level intrigue, while detecting evidence of double-dealing and counter-espionage. Spy thriller based on Len Deighton's novel, starring Michael Caine, Sue Lloyd, Gordon Jackson and Nigel Green

programme.title: DNA Family Secrets
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley helps people answer life-changing questions by examining their DNA. In the first episode, she comes to the aid of Richard, who wants to learn if a man claiming to be his biological father is the genuine article, and Charlie, who wants to know what her chances are of contracting the same terminal illness as her father

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team help people with a little land or a beautiful garden generate extra income by creating their own unique B&Bs. They begin by heading to the picturesque Wye Valley to help George and Al transform their battered old family camper van

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The designers work alone for the first time in the contest, each being assigned to transform a beach hut at Walton-on-the-Naze in Essex, in just two days on a budget of £1000. Working in a small space presents a serious challenge, and while some designers decide to incorporate existing features in their vision, others ruthlessly strip the huts bare in a bid to make their mark. Helping Michelle Ogundehin judge the results is journalist and interior designer Sophie Robinson

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the evening session on day two of the biennial event, held this year at Arena Torun in Poland. British pole-vaulter Holly Bradshaw will be hoping to go one better than in 2019 and emulate her 2013 triumph this evening, and medals are also decided in the women's long jump, 1500m and 400m, and the men's 400m and 60m. Another Team GB medal hope in Elliot Giles is also expected to be in action in the semi-finals of the 800m. With commentary from Steve Cram, Steve Backley and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Documentary about the change of musical direction in 1978, when pop singles were slowly ousted by disco, punk and new wave artists. The programme features contributions by members of the Boomtown Rats, Squeeze, Boney M and Sham 69 - among others - and archive performances by the likes of Elvis Costello, the Undertones, Ian Dury and the Blockheads and the Jam

programme.title: Dave Allen: The Immaculate Selection
programme.desc: A compilation of the finest, funniest and most irreverent routines by the Irish comedy writer and performer, known for his combination of laid-back storytelling and anger at social and political hypocrisy. The programme features a selection of BBC archive material originally recorded between 1971 and 1986

programme.title: Dave Allen Live: On Life
programme.desc: A selection of monologues by the Irish comedian, in which he reflects on the idiocies of life, meditating on everything from the process of ageing through to the invasive tendencies of the telephone

programme.title: Mindhorn (2016)
programme.desc: The star of a 1980s sci-fi cop show sets out to make it in Hollywood, but ends up with his life and career in ruins. Years later, he gets the chance to be a real hero when the police need his help. A dangerous fugitive is on the loose, who believes the character the actor played - a detective with a bionic eye that can detect lies - is real. Comedy, starring Julian Barratt, Essie Davis and Steve Coogan

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1978
programme.desc: A selection of classic songs from 1978, a year in which punk music and new wave rebellion co-existed with pop, disco, soul, reggae and prog rock. Featuring appearances on the show by Ian Dury, Elvis Costello, Blondie, Bob Marley, the Boomtown Rats, Kate Bush, Sham 69, Brotherhood of Man, Dan Hill, Boney M, 10cc and Manfred Mann's Earth Band

programme.title: Sailing
programme.desc: Live coverage of day two from Auckland, as defending champions Emirates Team New Zealand take on the winner of the PRADA Cup

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: A night-time visit to Britain's only colony of scorpions and finding out why the sea is salty. Presented by Nick Crane

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.desc: Documentary series exploring classic works of fiction, continuing with TV script writer Tony Jordan examining Charles Dickens' popular novel Great Expectations. He investigates why the author changed the ending of the book, and asks what this tells the reader about the writer. Plus, the original texts and manuscripts are examined, which offer clues about the novelist's troubled life, and may have influenced whether Pip would have a future with Estella in the story

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates soft purple sky and tall trees that almost seem to float in the hazy foothills of a distant, blue-grey mountain

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James takes Helen to a posh restaurant in his attempts to woo her, but when things do not quite go to plan, Tristan tries to brighten up James's love life by arranging a double blind date

programme.title: A United Kingdom (2016)
programme.desc: King Seretse Khama of Botswana put his kingdom into political and diplomatic turmoil after falling for Ruth Williams, a London office worker. Fact-based drama from Belle director Amma Asante, starring David Oyelowo and Rosamund Pike

programme.title: Life Cinematic
programme.desc: The Bafta-winning writer-director, whose movies include Belle and A United Kingdom, discusses the films that have influenced her career. Presented by Edith Bowman

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Treasures
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank's journey takes him to Japan and China, where he aims to discover whether ancient traditions and mysteries have survived in the modern age

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates soft purple sky and tall trees that almost seem to float in the hazy foothills of a distant, blue-grey mountain

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.desc: Documentary series exploring classic works of fiction, continuing with TV script writer Tony Jordan examining Charles Dickens' popular novel Great Expectations. He investigates why the author changed the ending of the book, and asks what this tells the reader about the writer. Plus, the original texts and manuscripts are examined, which offer clues about the novelist's troubled life, and may have influenced whether Pip would have a future with Estella in the story

programme.title: Hattie
programme.desc: Ruth Jones stars as Carry On actress Hattie Jacques in this dramatisation of her secret affair with a younger man in the early 1960s, while still married to actor John Le Mesurier. A love triangle develops between the three and Hattie uses her charm and ingenuity to control escalating family tensions and threats of public exposure - but must ultimately make a decision that puts her at risk of being left on her own. Also starring Robert Bathurst, Aidan Turner and Jeany Spark

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: Carly Simon discusses her life and career, reflecting on her time with ex-husband James Taylor, a heroin addict when they first met. She also recounts her number of highly public affairs in the early 1970s and how her experience fed into the 1972 album's most famous song, You're So Vain, performing the missing fourth verse on the piano - the first time she has ever sung it along with the melody. Plus, footage of Simon and Taylor Swift performing You're So Vain together, and extracts from an interview where Swift talks about her love for the song

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first aired on September 20, with performances by artists including Snap!, the Charlatans, the Farm, MC Tunes versus 808 State, Steve Miller Band, the Stone Roses and Twenty 4 Seven featuring Captain Hollywood

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Anthea Turner hosts the edition first aired on September 27, with performances by artists including Londonbeat, the Wedding Present, Status Quo, Bass-O-Matic, Maria McKee, Monie Love featuring True Image and Depeche Mode

programme.title: Joan Armatrading: Me Myself I
programme.desc: Known for her reclusiveness and barriers, the singer has for the first time granted access to her life and music. In this film she recounts her story from Caribbean emigre to being one of the most revered songwriters of her generation. The film covers Joan's childhood in Birmingham, her involvement in the musical Hair in 1968, her musical partnership with songwriter Pam Nestor and the relationship later forged with rock producer Glyn Johns. Plus, how she diversified her style in the 1980s, experimenting with synths and finding a new direction to create music without a producer

programme.title: The Kate Bush Story: Running Up That Hill
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the singer-songwriter's career and music from her 1978 debut single Wuthering Heights to her 2011 album 50 Words for Snow with testimony from collaborators, fellow artists inspired by her work and fans. Featuring David Gilmour, the guitarist who discovered Bush, as well as Peter Gabriel, Elton John, Stephen Fry, Outkast's Big Boi and Steve Coogan

programme.title: Aretha Franklin: Respect
programme.desc: A tribute to the singer known as the Queen of Soul, featuring contributions from Tom Jones, Beverley Knight, Clarke Peters and Trevor Nelson among others. Aretha was raised in Detroit where her father preached, and grew up singing gospel. Her career ignited when she signed to Atlantic in 1967 and global hits such as I Say a Little Prayer and Respect quickly established her in the soul genre, while her delivery and regal presence made her an iconic figure in the emerging Civil Rights movement. She renewed success in the 1980s when collaborating with Luther Vandross, cameoing in The Blues Brothers and then duetting with the likes of Annie Lennox and George Michael

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: A selection of performances by some of the most popular all-girl groups to have appeared on the show, from the pop-soul vocal ensembles of the 1960s to the glamorous bands of the new millennium. Includes songs by the Supremes, Labelle, TLC, Spice Girls, the Pointer Sisters and B*Witched

programme.title: Our Musical History
programme.desc: The duo reveal the songs that shaped their respective musical journeys. James picks Tina Turner's epic Proud Mary rendition as one of his all-time greats and Beverley introduces him to Big Mama Thornton - a woman who taught Elvis a thing a two before revealing how she felt when the opportunity to sing with her idol presented itself. Plus, the raw stadium rock vocal of Steve Tyler, the soaring acoustic purity of Eva Cassidy, the intensity of Otis Redding and the passion of Prince. Whitney Houston sings live to an audience of millions, Tom Jones returns to Wales to deliver one of his classic hits and a pitch-perfect George Michael serves to blow the roof off Wembley stadium

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: Carly Simon discusses her life and career, reflecting on her time with ex-husband James Taylor, a heroin addict when they first met. She also recounts her number of highly public affairs in the early 1970s and how her experience fed into the 1972 album's most famous song, You're So Vain, performing the missing fourth verse on the piano - the first time she has ever sung it along with the melody. Plus, footage of Simon and Taylor Swift performing You're So Vain together, and extracts from an interview where Swift talks about her love for the song

programme.title: Joan Armatrading: Me Myself I
programme.desc: Known for her reclusiveness and barriers, the singer has for the first time granted access to her life and music. In this film she recounts her story from Caribbean emigre to being one of the most revered songwriters of her generation. The film covers Joan's childhood in Birmingham, her involvement in the musical Hair in 1968, her musical partnership with songwriter Pam Nestor and the relationship later forged with rock producer Glyn Johns. Plus, how she diversified her style in the 1980s, experimenting with synths and finding a new direction to create music without a producer

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary charting the story of how film-makers used the Himalayan mountains for propaganda, as the world's great powers sent military-style expeditions to conquer the peaks between the 1920s and the 1960s. As a result, some of the range's most iconic mountaineering feats have been captured on film, including the preparations for Mallory and Irvine ill-fated ascent of Mount Everest in 1924, and the final conquest of the mountain by Hillary and Tenzing in 1953

programme.title: Himalaya with Michael Palin
programme.desc: Michael's journey takes him to Bhutan, where he enjoys a last glimpse of the Himalaya before heading to Paro to enjoy a Buddhist festival. Here he witnesses archery contests and the unfolding of a giant scroll during the night of a full moon

programme.title: Man in Room 301
programme.desc: Seppo travels to Greece, where Risto questions Niina about how well she knows Leo. In Finnish

programme.title: Man in Room 301
programme.desc: Olivia is frantic with worry when neither Anna nor Tytti return home from Sonia's party. In Greece, Kalle sneaks out of the hotel room to go climbing with Leo. In Finnish

programme.title: Vikings
programme.desc: Neil Oliver continues his attempt to shed new light on the Norse warriors by examining their sophisticated trading skills, and visits locations in Russia, Turkey and Ireland to explain how they established a business empire. He also explores the vast array of goods that were bought and sold by Viking merchants and settlers, including Chinese silks and Pictish slaves

programme.desc: In this episode, Dr Maggie reveals that no matter how much you plan, prepare and practise for space travel, things can sometimes go wrong. However, it didn’t go to plan – so what did he learn when things went wrong? And Dr Maggie answers viewers’ space questions and counts down the top five greatest achievements that you won’t have heard of! Buckle up, it’s time to go Out Of This World!

programme.desc: Join six-month-old Erin and Leo as they discover the world around them and start trying new foods for the first time. At home, we meet Erin and her family as everyone is playing and pretending to make phone calls on bananas. Meanwhile, Leo is playing on the swings at the local park, but soon it's time for his daily feed, while his sister stops to enjoy a delicious gingerbread man. After a short picnic, the family feeds the ducks and geese before Leo tries a rice cake for the very first time. At the petting farm, Erin and her family get the chance to feed some goats and hold a baby chick. The family have a picnic with juicy oranges and scrumptious sandwiches, while Erin has a bottle of milk. Leo’s family head home, where mum and Charlotte make a tasting platter for Leo, filled with fresh foods and a yummy yoghurt! At the dinner table, Erin’s mum dishes up a tasty meal - but will she like it?

Ferne meets some sun beetles, horned flour beetles and purple jewel beetles at the zoo, while Rory and his son Sandy look for ladybirds in the greenhouse and build a hibernation station for the garden. Plus, we find out why ladybirds are great for the environment. They are pest controllers, who eat lots of little bugs that can destroy valuable crops.

programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. Andy has to clear all the leaves off the path in the Amazon Dome, which is a huge task, but luckily Jen has invented a new machine to help him. It’s a superpowered leaf blower! Heading to Jungle Den to find it, Andy bumps into Mr Hammond, who is unpacking from his photography expedition to the Amazon River. He managed to photograph a particularly rare fish, which has not been named yet but belongs to a group of fish called cichlids. Mr Hammond has arranged a press conference to reveal his photo but can’t work out how to get the image off the memory card. Andy offers to drop the card over to Jen after he has finished clearing the Amazon Dome. The Go Jetters need to mount a manatee rescue quickly. While Xuli attempts to stop the speedboats, Foz launches the Vroomster. Tala asks her Uncle to shrink her, Foz and Lars with his size-ray. The tiny Go Jetters discover the ants will accept them as one of their own if they rub the insects’ smell on them. Now they are no longer a threat, the team follows the ants into their tree. Ubercorn and Glitch tuck into their picnic, but an ant steals the cherry from Glitch’s cake and takes it inside the tree. programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are going to Westman Islands, Iceland, where the world’s largest colony of Atlantic puffins gathers every year. Lars loves these tough little birds and explains how, although they spend most of their lives flying out to sea and fishing, they return to land each year to lay their eggs. When the baby birds, also called pufflings, eventually hatch, they fly out towards the ocean. But the light from nearby towns often confuses them about where to go, leading local children and adults to form 'puffin patrols' to rescue the pufflings. Lars is excited to help out but is upset when he discovers Glitch, also a fan of puffins, is already well-known on the islands for his skills as a puffin rescuer. Although Glitch offers him a place in his puffin patrol, Lars is determined to outdo Glitch with a patrol of his own - but when he recruits a clumsy Grimbot, things take a turn for the worse...

programme.title: Tommy Cooper at the BBC
programme.desc: Marking the centenary of his birth, Lenny Henry presents a selection of performances by the comedian. This collection features some of Cooper's most famous routines, and reveals that his reputation for terrible tricks that inevitably went wrong was all based on an act, as his on-stage bumbling persona hid a real talent as a magician

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The first quarter-final sees the four talented heat-winners return to fight for a place in knockout week in the Critic's Test. Their brief - set by Grace Dent, one of the country's toughest food critics - is to cook their take on a great British pudding. Presented by Gregg Wallace and John Torode

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea is horrified to learn Denise told Jack everything and is desperate to find him before he reports them. Whitney and Kush decide to go public with their relationship, and while Kat takes the news surprisingly well, Gray is clearly hiding his displeasure. Wise words from Mitch inspire Mick to make a decision, Ben encourages Phil to try online dating to find someone more suitable than Kat, and Dotty and Keegan are at loggerheads over Tiffany working as a hostess

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Under the eye of the acerbic chef, contestant pairs Chris and Luke, and Sian and Jacob answer a series of questions in order to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board - the more bars they build up, the more they can win up to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the more bars that accumulate, the greater the chance they will fall down requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve to triumph in a game show that's as unpredictable in nature as its host

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Line of Duty stars Vicky McClure and Adrian Dunbar talk about the hotly awaited sixth series of the BBC's crime drama. Star Wars' Daisy Ridley chats about starring in sci-fi adventure Chaos Walking, Breaking Bad's Bryan Cranston reflects on his role as a New Orleans judge in the TV drama Your Honor and Brit star Chiwetel Ejiofor promotes his romantic comedy heist, Locked Down. Plus, Imelda May performs Made to Love

programme.title: PRU
programme.desc: Halil, Hanna, Jaeden and Belle are four very different 15-year-olds who have one thing in common - they have all been permanently excluded from mainstream education and find themselves having to go through Year 10 together at their local Pupil Referral Unit. There, they are tutored by their long-suffering behaviour manager. One-off comedy, with Umit Ulgen, Kosar Ali, Michael Boahen, Jaye Eservas and Kerry Godliman

programme.title: Source Code (2011)
programme.desc: A military pilot is the subject of a top-secret experiment that transfers his mind into the body of a man killed in a terrorist bombing on a train, but only for the last eight minutes of the dead man's life. His mission is to use that time to find out who is responsible and if they intend to strike again. Sci-fi thriller, starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Michelle Monaghan, Vera Farmiga and Jeffrey Wright

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Gennaro Contaldo and Anna Jones, and guest Stacey Solomon. Drinks expert Helen McGinn joins via video link to pick wine to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Recipes That Made Me
programme.desc: Nisha Katona travels around the UK meeting passionate home cooks to discover how family recipes are passed down through generations from across the Indian Subcontinent. In the first episode, Nisha travels to the West Midlands to discover authentic family recipes from the Punjab, and learns about their love of bread, and makes tandoori chicken and spinach paneer

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents a look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Manchester City v Manchester United and Chelsea v Everton

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and David Harper help the red and blue teams find bargains in the antique shops of Dorking in Surrey, while host Anita Manning visits a Roman villa in West Sussex

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey, including a retro two-seater sofa and a vintage sewing box

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a spiritual businesswoman and her best friend to find a calming Hampshire home, and experiences the heights of Portsmouth's Spinnaker Tower

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal returns with the culinary duel in which two chefs create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought on to the show by the contestants assisting each of them. In the first edition, Jeremy Pang and Akis Petretzikis are joined by brothers Justin and Adrian

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers Joe Pasquale, Grace Dent, Mwaksy and Robin Ince to take to the black chair and play for charity to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions. The contestants' chosen subjects are the Planet of the Apes movies, Edward VI, The Gospel of Mark, and The Young Ones

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a new family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees two twin brothers from Leeds answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes, which they plan to use to further their education and provide some help for their mother. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as past and present cast members of Casualty take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Di Botcher, George Rainsford, Ian Bleasdale, Sue Devaney, Azuka Oforka, James Redmond, Clive Mantle and Julia Watson

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jan receives a call demanding she steal drugs from the hospital and smuggle them into prison. When a patient she knows dies, her sadness is tempered by the opportunity the situation presents to obtain the drugs she needs. Connie lashes out in her frustration over Jacob's new relationship, and a friendly bet between Rosa and Charlie soon gets out of control

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Denton fights for her life as disturbing details emerge of a conspiracy involving corrupt officers, and Arnott uncovers startling new details about the detective's personal life which appear to support her claim that she is being framed. Meanwhile, Hastings puts his career on the line to pursue a high-level target. Crime thriller, starring Adrian Dunbar and Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Aston Villa v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Villa Park and Brighton & Hove Albion v Leicester City at Amex Stadium. The Villans were looking to do the double over their Midlands rivals after prevailing 1-0 at Molineux in December, while Leicester were hoping to boost their top four hopes with a third win of the season over the Seagulls, having defeated them at home in the league and FA Cup. Plus, Burnley v Arsenal and Sheffield United v Southampton

programme.title: Margin Call (2011)
programme.desc: A bitter executive is dismissed from an investment bank, but plans his revenge by handing secret information on the company's fortunes to a junior analyst. When the information is decoded, it reveals that the company is on the brink of a devastating financial crisis, a revelation that is soon spreading throughout the workforce. Drama, starring Kevin Spacey, Paul Bettany and Jeremy Irons

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts heads to Newport in south Wales to see a plot of land with no planning permission, while Jacqui Joseph is in Eastleigh, Hampshire, to visit a three-bed mid-terrace, and Dion Dublin visits a one-bedroom bungalow in Lichfield, Staffordshire, that requires change of use to make it a residential dwelling again

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste meet a woman whose online friend tried to gain her trust and affection to fleece her out of thousands of pounds

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Malvern in Worcestershire, where Gary Pe and John Cameron are the experts assisting the two sides

programme.title: First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Kirsten Campbell presents live coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Luca is backed into a corner when his past comes back to haunt him, Bear and Sid go on a double date, and Karen meets a mother who is desperate to save her sick daughter

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu are hired to locate a photograph stolen from a local pub, whose owner is in the middle of a feud with another local family - only for events to take a deadly turn

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Steve Brown takes a couple round the West Yorkshire market town of Wetherby, showing them properties for their £550,000 budget and also exploring the local cafes and horticulture

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Engaged couple Nick and Donnie compete with the help of chefs Jeremy Pang and Anna Haugh, seeing who can come up with the best meal against the clock. Hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Sellers try to get a deal for a painting by early 20th-century artist William Russell Flint, a tin rocket toy, a set of retro formica furniture, and a carved Japanese walking stick

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Scottish National Party

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest, presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat feels uneasy when Phil suggests that next time they should meet up in a hotel, worried that things are getting a little too serious for her liking. Lily confronts Ruby about the miscarriage, telling her it was not Stacey's fault. Denise suggests asking Jack for help with the family's problems - but Chelsea does not like the thought of him sniffing around - and Keegan is not happy to hear about Tiffany and Dotty's big money-making plan

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents the final of the contest, which sees the three remaining participants aiming to become top dog-groomer - and win themselves the Golden Stanley trophy to prove it. All they have to do is execute one of the toughest challenges in the industry - a continental clip on an elegant standard poodle. As if that were not enough, they have to guess a breed of dog while blindfolded, before one last dogwalk parade. Who will impress judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor the most?

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Contestant pairs Latia and Rose, and Chris and Luke answer a series of questions to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board - and the more bars they build up, the more they can win to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the higher they go, the greater the chance of falling, requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of guests to answer questions from a virtual audience. Her panellists this week are Secretary of State for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy Kwasi Kwarteng MP, Shadow Foreign Secretary Lisa Nandy MP, founder and CEO of Palmer Automative Andy Palmer, economist and Chief Executive of the New Economics Forum Miatta Fahnbulleh and businessman and entrepreneur Theo Paphitis

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring all the goals, analysis of the talking points and post-match reaction from managers and players. All of the day's games will be covered, with the panel of former professionals analysing the tactics employed by the teams, as well as highlighting any deficiencies that led to a team's downfall

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Ochuko Ojiri lead two teams competing at Ardingly in West Sussex, while presenter Christina Trevanion looks at the names given to collectors of different antiques

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: National Crime Agency officers seize cash and drugs from a suspected County Lines drug dealer, while Border Force officers at Gatwick sell off a huge haul of gold seized from a passenger. Presented by Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste report on a woman suffering from what she felt was a type of Stockholm syndrome, in that she was unable to escape a relationship with a man who took tens of thousands of pounds from her

programme.title: Coronavirus: Scotland's Response
programme.desc: All the latest developments as Scotland responds to the coronavirus pandemic, plus answers to the many questions you have during these unprecedented times

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu delve into the ultra-competitive world of private tutoring, while a reunion with an old Rada friend, presents Sebastian with a choice about his future that could change everything for the agency. Tamzin Outhwaite guest stars, with Mark Benton and Jo Joyner

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Korean food is set to be the next big thing in the UK, so bikers Dave Myers and Si King conclude their culinary journey around Asia by finding out more about it. Out with some young Seoulites, they discover the Korean barbecue and cook for a Korean pop star. After a lesson in making kimchi, the bikers head south for a local squid festival.

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman visits the seaside town of Ayr, combing through its good-looking property stock and popular culture haunts. With £400,000 in their pocket, her buyers have pinpointed Ayr as a potential perfect town because it puts them a walk from the beach and a train ride from the buzz of Glasgow. They soon discover it's a real gem of a place, with a multi-generational crowd thanks to its university campus and lively theatre scene, as well as award-winning local seafood on the waterfront

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Romantic novelists Mandy and Rachel do battle in the kitchen, with the help of chefs Akis Petretzikis and Ellis Barrie. The duo are tasked to cook against the clock, producing dishes based on a budget of £5, from tomato carpaccio with baked feta to halloumi saganaki. However, they need to be on their mettle when the 10-Minute Challenge throws up tinned ham and treacle. Hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Dealers Adi Higham, James Gooch, James Broad, Lucy Ryder Richardson and Tash Francis compete to buy an ornate clock by the distinguished British horologist Charles Frodsham, an antique tombola machine, a 19th-century armchair and a vintage coffee set. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Martin Clunes round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Lenny's mission to pacify Tyler misses the deadline - and the Foulkes aren't a family to be disrespected without consequence. With Cameron missing on bail, Poppy has to decide if she can stay and fight her stalker again
