programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Korean food is set to be the next big thing in the UK, so bikers Dave Myers and Si King conclude their culinary journey around Asia by finding out more about it. Out with some young Seoulites, they discover the Korean barbecue and cook for a Korean pop star. After a lesson in making kimchi, the bikers head south for a local squid festival.

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman visits the seaside town of Ayr, combing through its good-looking property stock and popular culture haunts. With £400,000 in their pocket, her buyers have pinpointed Ayr as a potential perfect town because it puts them a walk from the beach and a train ride from the buzz of Glasgow. They soon discover it's a real gem of a place, with a multi-generational crowd thanks to its university campus and lively theatre scene, as well as award-winning local seafood on the waterfront

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Romantic novelists Mandy and Rachel do battle in the kitchen, with the help of chefs Akis Petretzikis and Ellis Barrie. The duo are tasked to cook against the clock, producing dishes based on a budget of £5, from tomato carpaccio with baked feta to halloumi saganaki. However, they need to be on their mettle when the 10-Minute Challenge throws up tinned ham and treacle. Hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Dealers Adi Higham, James Gooch, James Broad, Lucy Ryder Richardson and Tash Francis compete to buy an ornate clock by the distinguished British horologist Charles Frodsham, an antique tombola machine, a 19th-century armchair and a vintage coffee set. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Martin Clunes round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Tommy Cooper at the BBC
programme.desc: Marking the centenary of his birth, Lenny Henry presents a selection of performances by the comedian. This collection features some of Cooper's most famous routines, and reveals that his reputation for terrible tricks that inevitably went wrong was all based on an act, as his on-stage bumbling persona hid a real talent as a magician

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The first quarter-final sees the four talented heat-winners return to fight for a place in knockout week in the Critic's Test. Their brief - set by Grace Dent, one of the country's toughest food critics - is to cook their take on a great British pudding. Presented by Gregg Wallace and John Torode

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea is horrified to learn Denise told Jack everything and is desperate to find him before he reports them. Whitney and Kush decide to go public with their relationship, and while Kat takes the news surprisingly well, Gray is clearly hiding his displeasure. Wise words from Mitch inspire Mick to make a decision, Ben encourages Phil to try online dating to find someone more suitable than Kat, and Dotty and Keegan are at loggerheads over Tiffany working as a hostess

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Under the eye of the acerbic chef, contestant pairs Chris and Luke, and Sian and Jacob answer a series of questions in order to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board - the more bars they build up, the more they can win up to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the more bars that accumulate, the greater the chance they will fall down requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve to triumph in a game show that's as unpredictable in nature as its host

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Line of Duty stars Vicky McClure and Adrian Dunbar talk about the hotly awaited sixth series of the BBC's crime drama. Star Wars' Daisy Ridley chats about starring in sci-fi adventure Chaos Walking, Breaking Bad's Bryan Cranston reflects on his role as a New Orleans judge in the TV drama Your Honor and Brit star Chiwetel Ejiofor promotes his romantic comedy heist, Locked Down. Plus, Imelda May performs Made to Love

programme.title: PRU
programme.desc: Halil, Hanna, Jaeden and Belle are four very different 15-year-olds who have one thing in common - they have all been permanently excluded from mainstream education and find themselves having to go through Year 10 together at their local Pupil Referral Unit. There, they are tutored by their long-suffering behaviour manager. One-off comedy, with Umit Ulgen, Kosar Ali, Michael Boahen, Jaye Eservas and Kerry Godliman

programme.title: Source Code (2011)
programme.desc: A military pilot is the subject of a top-secret experiment that transfers his mind into the body of a man killed in a terrorist bombing on a train, but only for the last eight minutes of the dead man's life. His mission is to use that time to find out who is responsible and if they intend to strike again. Sci-fi thriller, starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Michelle Monaghan, Vera Farmiga and Jeffrey Wright

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Gennaro Contaldo and Anna Jones, and guest Stacey Solomon. Drinks expert Helen McGinn joins via video link to pick wine to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Recipes That Made Me
programme.desc: Nisha Katona travels around the UK meeting passionate home cooks to discover how family recipes are passed down through generations from across the Indian Subcontinent. In the first episode, Nisha travels to the West Midlands to discover authentic family recipes from the Punjab, and learns about their love of bread, and makes tandoori chicken and spinach paneer

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents a look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Manchester City v Manchester United and Chelsea v Everton

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and David Harper help the red and blue teams find bargains in the antique shops of Dorking in Surrey, while host Anita Manning visits a Roman villa in West Sussex

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey, including a retro two-seater sofa and a vintage sewing box

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a spiritual businesswoman and her best friend to find a calming Hampshire home, and experiences the heights of Portsmouth's Spinnaker Tower

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal returns with the culinary duel in which two chefs create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought on to the show by the contestants assisting each of them. In the first edition, Jeremy Pang and Akis Petretzikis are joined by brothers Justin and Adrian

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers Joe Pasquale, Grace Dent, Mwaksy and Robin Ince to take to the black chair and play for charity to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions. The contestants' chosen subjects are the Planet of the Apes movies, Edward VI, The Gospel of Mark, and The Young Ones

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a new family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees two twin brothers from Leeds answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes, which they plan to use to further their education and provide some help for their mother. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as past and present cast members of Casualty take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Di Botcher, George Rainsford, Ian Bleasdale, Sue Devaney, Azuka Oforka, James Redmond, Clive Mantle and Julia Watson

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jan receives a call demanding she steal drugs from the hospital and smuggle them into prison. When a patient she knows dies, her sadness is tempered by the opportunity the situation presents to obtain the drugs she needs. Connie lashes out in her frustration over Jacob's new relationship, and a friendly bet between Rosa and Charlie soon gets out of control

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Denton fights for her life as disturbing details emerge of a conspiracy involving corrupt officers, and Arnott uncovers startling new details about the detective's personal life which appear to support her claim that she is being framed. Meanwhile, Hastings puts his career on the line to pursue a high-level target. Crime thriller, starring Adrian Dunbar and Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Aston Villa v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Villa Park and Brighton & Hove Albion v Leicester City at Amex Stadium. The Villans were looking to do the double over their Midlands rivals after prevailing 1-0 at Molineux in December, while Leicester were hoping to boost their top four hopes with a third win of the season over the Seagulls, having defeated them at home in the league and FA Cup. Plus, Burnley v Arsenal and Sheffield United v Southampton

programme.title: Margin Call (2011)
programme.desc: A bitter executive is dismissed from an investment bank, but plans his revenge by handing secret information on the company's fortunes to a junior analyst. When the information is decoded, it reveals that the company is on the brink of a devastating financial crisis, a revelation that is soon spreading throughout the workforce. Drama, starring Kevin Spacey, Paul Bettany and Jeremy Irons

programme.title: Scottish Parliament
programme.desc: Committee session of the Scottish Parliament's inquiry into the Scottish government's handling of harassment complaints, with evidence from the first minister of Scotland, Nicola Sturgeon, from Wednesday 3 March.

programme.desc: Questions in the Lords to UK government ministers, from Wednesday 3 March

programme.title: 'S E Iasg a Th'Annam (I'm a Fish)
programme.desc: The Blue Dragon Fish tells us how she is a Sea Slug and that she loves to float on the surface of the ocean

programme.title: Shane an Chef
programme.desc: Shane gets an idea from Mama Polenta for something different for the menu

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: The friends are sent in search of a rocking horse

programme.title: Rita is Crogall
programme.desc: Rita loves to ride her bicycle round the park, but it’s not that easy without stabilisers

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Catriona MacPhee reads A Bhith nam Leòmhann written and illustrated by Emma Carlisle and translated into Gaelic by Jo Macdonald

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: One of DM's flying kicks damages the holodeck, and he and Penfold wake to find they have been in a three-year dream, that they aren't secret agents and work in IT instead

programme.title: Ronia, Nighean a' Mheirlich/Ronja, the Robber's Daughter
programme.desc: Ronia and Biorc have promised one another to be like sister and brother, but when she is ill and stuck indoors, Ronia feels worried that she isn’t able to see Biorc

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Olaidh Orains and Coinneachd Clogaid compete as always, but Olaidh has it in the bag

programme.title: Port Pàipeir
programme.desc: Matilda has the power to excel in business, being able to make calculations and predict market behaviour. Tropecio Ferguson requests her help for his new 'harmless' business

programme.title: Turas a' Bhradain
programme.desc: Neen MacKay explores Scotland's fishing rivers, focusing on the nation's hydro river systems, which are respected the world over

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Documentary about caring individuals, including nurses

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Eòrpa
programme.desc: The use of new national materials to teach personal education in the Western Isles, which has been the focus of much debate among, parents, church leaders and councillors

programme.title: Sruth
programme.desc: Pàdruig Morrison from Grimsay in the Western Isles performs with Bríd Harper, the Co Donegal musician and one of the finest Irish traditional fiddlers of her generation

programme.title: Iùl a' Chiùil (The Musical Path)
programme.desc: Exploring innovation in Irish and Scottish traditional music, charting the shifting sands between tradition and innovation over the past half -century and featuring some of the best new, cutting-edge acts including Zoe Conway and John McIntyre; Chris Stout and Catriona Mackay, and Rónán Ó Snodaigh

programme.title: Eileanan na Banrighe/Islands of the Queen
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the diversity of the islands of the Commonwealth. From weathered stone huts in the in upland moors to golden Caribbean beaches, the series shows the isles in all their contrast and glory

programme.title: FilmG
programme.desc: Filmed and edited by students from the Nicolson Institute in Stornoway during their 2019 summer holidays, An-Dràsta! presents a local perspective on a global crisis, examining how the climate emergency is affecting the Outer Hebrides

programme.title: Na Millenni-Gaels
programme.desc: Anton and Alisha are now parents to a little baby girl. They take her to meet the children in the nursery, who are amazed at how small she is and form some interesting ideas

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Leugh le Linda
programme.desc: Linda Macleod travels to the Isle of Lewis to meet nursery school pupils and read Geàrr agus Sligeanach, written and illustrated by Alison Murray and translated by Dolina MacLeod

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean (Puffin Rock)
programme.desc: Baba plans to play with his new friend, the snail - until the little creature disappears

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.desc: Micawber is in need of a bath, but first Pàdraig must get the bath to the lighthouse

programme.title: Nannag a' Noo/Huggleboo
programme.desc: Huggleboo is bored but dad wants to play with her, and together they build a castle in Huggleboo’s room

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Martin MacIntyre reads An Crogall fon Leabaidh written and illustrated by Judith Kerr and translated into Gaelic by Donald John Macleod

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: Piers the puffin is on a mission to find himself a mate and learn the secret of catching 12 fish at once to feed his family. Life as a new recruit is tough and the rookie makes every mistake in the book. Will he escape the hungry seagulls and peregrines, and will his first chick learn to fly by winter?

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Coinneach Clogaid is up to his usual bad behaviour

programme.title: An Rud As Fheàrr Leam
programme.desc: Esther from Lewis tells us about her favourite hobbies, which include playing the piano and archery

programme.title: Na Moomins/Moomin Valley
programme.desc: Papa wants the family to start a new life and adventure on a remote island, but how will things work out for them?

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: Herman Lee decides to make a bit of extra money by faking crop circles, but receives a nasty surprise when an unexpected visitor turns up to tell him he is contravening copyright

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Cearcall
programme.desc: The 1970s conflict between creel fishermen and trawlers off the coast of Skye. Plus, employment initiatives intended to get young people into work. Presented by Donald Morrison

programme.title: Gàrraidhean Mòra na h-Alba
programme.desc: Murdo Macdonald visits Scone Palace Gardens, known most famously perhaps for Moot Hill - the ancient crowning place of the Kings of Scots

programme.title: Iùl a' Chiùil (The Musical Path)
programme.desc: The rise of Celtic music in the '70s, '80s and '90s, led by bands like Runrig, Clannad, Moving Hearts and Capercaillie. Plus, the extraordinary impact of Riverdance

programme.title: State of Happiness
programme.desc: Mayor Rettedal uses unorthodox methods to get Stavanger elected as the oil capital of Norway, while Anna does something she regrets at a party at the Esso Motor Hotel, and Toril reaches out to Bengt Hamre. In Norwegian and English

programme.title: ALT
programme.desc: Including a live performance by The Skids, an eclectic tune from Makeness, and Claire Grogan chats about her career highlights

programme.title: Seirm
programme.desc: A selection of tracks from the world of trad and folk music, including Shooglenifty and Ross Ainslie

programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Ceitidh Morag (Katie Morag)
programme.desc: Spring arrives, and the youngster is very fond of the new lambs

programme.title: 'S E Iasg a Th'Annam (I'm a Fish)
programme.desc: Focusing on the boxfish, who live alone on the reefs off the north coast of Australia and in the Pacific and Indian Oceans

programme.title: Tree Fu Tom
programme.desc: Tom and Twigs invite two dark sprites to come above ground with them, promising to shield them with robotoc shadows. However, they run into trouble when the robot runs out of power, exposing their visitors to the sunlight

programme.desc: Fuss and excitement follows a period of calm

programme.title: Port Pàipeir
programme.desc: Matilda has the power to excel in business, being able to make calculations and predict market behaviour. Tropecio Ferguson requests her help for his new 'harmless' business

programme.title: An Rud As Fheàrr Leam
programme.desc: Esther from Lewis tells us about her favourite hobbies, which include playing the piano and archery

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Scottish Premiership Football
programme.desc: Aberdeen v Hamilton Academical. A chance to see the top-flight clash from Pittodrie. With commentary from Alex O'Henley

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: Bruce MacGregor, Liz Doherty, Mhairi Hall and Mike Vass gather in Inverness to lend musical weight to Falls of Lora

programme.title: Cearcall
programme.desc: Donald Morrison takes a nostalgic look at material from the popular Gaelic current affairs series. This edition reflects on the development scheme IDP and includes a report on Rhenigidale in Harris, which didn't even have a road at the time

programme.title: Gaisgich Oga an Darna Cogaidh
programme.desc: Series focusing on children of courage. The opening episode centres on 1938 in Germany and on 10-year-old Anton, who wants to join the Hitler Youth

programme.title: Seirm
programme.desc: A selection of tracks from the world of trad and folk music, including Gnoss, Rachel Newton and De Temps Antan

programme.title: Eileanan na Banrighe/Islands of the Queen
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the diversity of the islands of the Commonwealth. From weathered stone huts in the in upland moors to golden Caribbean beaches, the series shows the isles in all their contrast and glory

programme.title: FilmG
programme.desc: The bean-nighe, or washing woman, is well-known to her neighbours, but they suspect that she is doing more for them than just their laundry

programme.title: Scottish Premiership Football
programme.desc: Aberdeen v Hamilton Academical. A chance to see the top-flight clash from Pittodrie. With commentary from Alex O'Henley

programme.title: ALT
programme.desc: Highlights from the music show, including a flavour of live performance from Travis at the the QMU in Glasgow, plus chat from Fran Healey and Dougie Payne from the band

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news, current affairs and entertainment, including Richard Arnold chatting to Hollywood stars Eddie Murphy and Arsenio Hall about their screen return in Coming 2 America, in which they reprise their roles as King Akeem and Semmi

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: A team of colleagues from the British Transport Police compete in the game show, taking it in turns to find the answers to top 10 lists, before trying to score a perfect 10 in the final round. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Jill, Beorn, Benny and Annie pit their wits against the Chaser. Will it be a case of winner takes it all for the quartet or will they reach their waterloo against the quiz genius?

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Al fires Priya, Charity forms a plan and Lydia tries to bury her feelings

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: Julie Etchingham asks whether the United Kingdom is on the brink of a break-up. Using polls of English, Scottish, Welsh and Northern Irish voters, the programme discovers how people feel about the union. Julia examines they way Scots are being driven toward independence, and why continuing issues around Brexit are making people in Northern Ireland question their role in the UK

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Charity is in trouble, Cain gets emotional and Ellis and Priya get close

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programme.desc: Fresh from the Government's budget announcements, Martin takes a look at what it all means for family finances. Plus, more News You Can Use and the latest information on savings and consumer rights

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Piers chats to actor and director Rupert Everett, who reflects on the caustic comments he has made about several co-stars, including Madonna and Julia Roberts. He also talks about the promiscuous life he led in the 1970s and 1980s, the impact of the HIV/Aids crisis, and discusses his romantic relationships with women, including TV presenter Paula Yates

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: 56 Up
programme.desc: The concluding part of the 2012 update in the landmark documentary series, following the stories of a group of participants whose lives have been chronicled since 1964. Having last spoken to the cameras in 2005, the participants, who are now 56, talk to Michael Apted about the highs and lows of getting older, reveal whether they have achieved their ambitions and reflect on their appearances in previous instalments of the programme

programme.desc: The master baker who turns out a traditional regional delicacy with a Greek twist and the craftsmen who turn raw hides into handcrafted saddles

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Ric, Gemma, Cameron and Michele work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Gabby is dejected, Priya battles her conflicting emotions and Chas is unimpressed

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Hour-long episode. Leanne finds Jacob and Simon counting their drug money, and when she objects, Jacob makes a dig about her dead son. She begs Simon to stay away from Jacob and stop dealing, but Simon points out the stark truth - they need the money. Carla promises to help Leanne, but doesn't want Peter involved

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective reopens an unsolved case when a skeleton is discovered beneath the burned-out remains of a Whitley Bay nightclub. She suspects the bones belong to a notorious criminal who was once a friend of her father and has been missing for more than 20 years. When another murder occurs nearby, she investigates the possibility that a local crime family may be involved with both deaths, while the original owners of the club also have secrets to hide. Brenda Blethyn stars in an adaptation of Ann Cleeves' 2017 Vera Stanhope novel The Seagull

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: Return of the drama about two chalk-and-cheese police partners solving crimes on the streets of Bath. In the first episode, five friends take a hot-air balloon trip but only four come back down alive, leaving McDonald and Dodds to untangle a complex web of lies spanning 30 years. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia star, with guest appearances from Rob Brydon, Rupert Graves, Martin Kemp and Patsy Kensit

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Cole finds himself on the rebound after Natalie's engagement, and his new romantic interest, Julie has an unexpected connection to Assistant District Attorney Erica, complicating things at work. Meanwhile, Murtaugh worries about the connection between the murder he's investigating and a new opportunity for Trish to serve as co-counsel on a high-profile lawsuit alongside Leo Getz. Action drama, starring Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Instructed to keep an eye on Gran while the rest of the Spuds visit to the doctor, Dave finds that she has escaped.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave is practising for Grimsby's Grimmest Face competition and finds that the wind changing direction really can cause the face to stick...

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The Tracy brothers and their friends at International Rescue set out to foil the nefarious plots of the evil Hood. An updated remake of the classic 1960s marionette adventure series, this time featuring computer-animated characters on live-action modeled sets. With the voices of Rosamund Pike, Kayvan Novak and Thomas Brodie-Sangster, with the original Parker - David Graham - returning to voice Lady Penelope's long-serving chauffeur

programme.title: Mission Employable
programme.desc: Agent Danny visits the Stadium of Light where he gets the chance to train with the ladies' team at Sunderland AFC

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: One of the Queen's corgis takes a liking to Bean's teddy and runs off with it during a visit to Buckingham Palace

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: A mysterious furry pink animal moves in with Bean, who doesn't know what it is but can't bring himself to get rid of such an odd creature

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: The raccoons think they will finally be able to steal something from Mike's house when they handcuff Mike's back legs together.

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: The Paws of Destiny
programme.desc: Po and the Kids are reunited with Li, Grandma, Ping, Rooster, Wing, and Wong on Volcano Island - they then work together to find out what's really going on the island. Meanwhile, Xiao becomes suspicious of some of her royal workers...

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave and his friends get lost on a school orienteering exercise thanks to Robert Robot, who is unable to read the map - snatched by a flock of giant tiger pigeons and deposited into their equally giant nest, Dave must retrieve the map from Robert and get them all back to school.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by guest Priyanka Chopra Jonas and chefs Claude Bosi and Stephen Terry. Plus, recipes for pizza pie, harissa chicken and mac and cheese

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef transforms classic dishes with herbs and spices from his garden, including moules three ways and a slow-roasted harissa lamb shoulder with chickpeas

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of three meetings, including the 1.15 Greatwood Gold Cup from Newbury, the 3.15 Morebattle Hurdle from Kelso and the 3.30 Grimthorpe Chase from Doncaster. With analysis from Mick Fitzgerald, Luke Harvey and Alice Plunkett, reports by Natalie Green, and commentary from Mark Johnson

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. A pensioner reveals too much about his sex life, a bear commits a carjack, and a man wakes up when his wife sets the house on fire

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard invites Newsnight presenter Kirsty Wark, actor/comedian Miles Jupp and TV personality Kerry Katona to take part in a special edition of the quiz show. Contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking Strictly head judge Shirley Ballas, actress Bhavna Limbachia and This Morning's resident GP Dr Ranj Singh to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. The players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for their chosen charities

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway
programme.desc: Comedian and actor Rob Delaney is this week's guest announcer, TV presenter Davina McCall is the latest victim of Get Out of Me Ear, and singer Tom Jones is in the studio for an End of the Show Show with a difference. Plus, Stephen Mulhern is back for an Ant v Dec challenge and another lucky viewer gets the chance to Win the Ads

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the first semi-final, as 12 acts that made it through the blind auditions and the battle rounds compete to impress the judges and win a place in the final. Coaches will.i.am, Tom Jones, Olly Murs and Anne-Marie all return to their infamous red chairs as the acts they have chosen go head to head and fight for their place on their coach's team. The singers that impress their coach the most remain in the competition with a chance of winning a recording contract. Will those that aren't so lucky be stolen by another coach or will they be going home?

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show: Special Guests
programme.desc: A look back at some of the most memorable moments from recent series of the talk show. Among the guests featured are Madonna, Dave Bautista, Mel Giedroyc, Peter Kay, the Spice Girls, Harrison Ford, Glenn Close, Mo Gilligan, Keira Knightley and Bear Grylls

programme.title: Die Another Day (2002)
programme.desc: James Bond is suspended from duty after being taken hostage in North Korea, but goes against orders to track down those who betrayed him. During the pursuit of the terrorist he was investigating before his capture, he discovers a connection with a British billionaire who has constructed a devastating orbital weapon. Spy adventure, starring Pierce Brosnan, Halle Berry, Toby Stephens, Judi Dench and John Cleese

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the supermarket-based game show, inviting three pairs of shoppers to 'go wild in the aisles' as they compete in the ultimate supermarket challenge to win a £3,000 prize. New quiz rounds feature alongside the legendary trolley dashes and the most famous pastel jumpers on television

She begs Simon to stay away from Jacob and stop dealing, but Simon points out the stark truth - they need the money. Carla promises to help Leanne, but doesn't want Peter involved

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective reopens an unsolved case when a skeleton is discovered beneath the burned-out remains of a Whitley Bay nightclub. She suspects the bones belong to a notorious criminal who was once a friend of her father and has been missing for more than 20 years. When another murder occurs nearby, she investigates the possibility that a local crime family may be involved with both deaths, while the original owners of the club also have secrets to hide. Brenda Blethyn stars in an adaptation of Ann Cleeves' 2017 Vera Stanhope novel The Seagull

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: Return of the drama about two chalk-and-cheese police partners solving crimes on the streets of Bath. In the first episode, five friends take a hot-air balloon trip but only four come back down alive, leaving McDonald and Dodds to untangle a complex web of lies spanning 30 years. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia star, with guest appearances from Rob Brydon, Rupert Graves, Martin Kemp and Patsy Kensit

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Cole finds himself on the rebound after Natalie's engagement, and his new romantic interest, Julie has an unexpected connection to Assistant District Attorney Erica, complicating things at work. Meanwhile, Murtaugh worries about the connection between the murder he's investigating and a new opportunity for Trish to serve as co-counsel on a high-profile lawsuit alongside Leo Getz. Action drama, starring Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Instructed to keep an eye on Gran while the rest of the Spuds visit to the doctor, Dave finds that she has escaped.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave is practising for Grimsby's Grimmest Face competition and finds that the wind changing direction really can cause the face to stick...

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The Tracy brothers and their friends at International Rescue set out to foil the nefarious plots of the evil Hood. An updated remake of the classic 1960s marionette adventure series, this time featuring computer-animated characters on live-action modeled sets. With the voices of Rosamund Pike, Kayvan Novak and Thomas Brodie-Sangster, with the original Parker - David Graham - returning to voice Lady Penelope's long-serving chauffeur

programme.title: Mission Employable
programme.desc: Agent Danny visits the Stadium of Light where he gets the chance to train with the ladies' team at Sunderland AFC

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: One of the Queen's corgis takes a liking to Bean's teddy and runs off with it during a visit to Buckingham Palace

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: A mysterious furry pink animal moves in with Bean, who doesn't know what it is but can't bring himself to get rid of such an odd creature

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: The raccoons think they will finally be able to steal something from Mike's house when they handcuff Mike's back legs together.

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: The Paws of Destiny
programme.desc: Po and the Kids are reunited with Li, Grandma, Ping, Rooster, Wing, and Wong on Volcano Island - they then work together to find out what's really going on the island. Meanwhile, Xiao becomes suspicious of some of her royal workers...

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave and his friends get lost on a school orienteering exercise thanks to Robert Robot, who is unable to read the map - snatched by a flock of giant tiger pigeons and deposited into their equally giant nest, Dave must retrieve the map from Robert and get them all back to school.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by guest Priyanka Chopra Jonas and chefs Claude Bosi and Stephen Terry. Plus, recipes for pizza pie, harissa chicken and mac and cheese

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef transforms classic dishes with herbs and spices from his garden, including moules three ways and a slow-roasted harissa lamb shoulder with chickpeas

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of three meetings, including the 1.15 Greatwood Gold Cup from Newbury, the 3.15 Morebattle Hurdle from Kelso and the 3.30 Grimthorpe Chase from Doncaster. With analysis from Mick Fitzgerald, Luke Harvey and Alice Plunkett, reports by Natalie Green, and commentary from Mark Johnson

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. A pensioner reveals too much about his sex life, a bear commits a carjack, and a man wakes up when his wife sets the house on fire

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard invites Newsnight presenter Kirsty Wark, actor/comedian Miles Jupp and TV personality Kerry Katona to take part in a special edition of the quiz show. Contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking Strictly head judge Shirley Ballas, actress Bhavna Limbachia and This Morning's resident GP Dr Ranj Singh to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. The players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for their chosen charities

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway
programme.desc: Comedian and actor Rob Delaney is this week's guest announcer, TV presenter Davina McCall is the latest victim of Get Out of Me Ear, and singer Tom Jones is in the studio for an End of the Show Show with a difference. Plus, Stephen Mulhern is back for an Ant v Dec challenge and another lucky viewer gets the chance to Win the Ads

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the first semi-final, as 12 acts that made it through the blind auditions and the battle rounds compete to impress the judges and win a place in the final. Coaches will.i.am, Tom Jones, Olly Murs and Anne-Marie all return to their infamous red chairs as the acts they have chosen go head to head and fight for their place on their coach's team. The singers that impress their coach the most remain in the competition with a chance of winning a recording contract. Will those that aren't so lucky be stolen by another coach or will they be going home?

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show: Special Guests
programme.desc: A look back at some of the most memorable moments from recent series of the talk show. Among the guests featured are Madonna, Dave Bautista, Mel Giedroyc, Peter Kay, the Spice Girls, Harrison Ford, Glenn Close, Mo Gilligan, Keira Knightley and Bear Grylls

programme.title: Die Another Day (2002)
programme.desc: James Bond is suspended from duty after being taken hostage in North Korea, but goes against orders to track down those who betrayed him. During the pursuit of the terrorist he was investigating before his capture, he discovers a connection with a British billionaire who has constructed a devastating orbital weapon. Spy adventure, starring Pierce Brosnan, Halle Berry, Toby Stephens, Judi Dench and John Cleese

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth finds himself in interesting company as English socialites and American millionaires rub shoulders on board a train bound for the French Riviera. Passengers include oil magnate Rufus Van Aldin (Elliott Gould) and his daughter Ruth (Jaime Murray) - to whom he has given a magnificent ruby known as the Heart of Fire. With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A visiting American academic delivers a controversial speech, with many audience members worried his ideas could be used to target ethnic minorities. So when he is found hanged the next morning and Dr Hobson quickly discounts suicide, Lewis and Hathaway realise they have many suspects - from the local anti-racism activist who was first in voicing her disgust at the professor's beliefs to the women who had bombarded him with threatening e-mails. But then another body turns up and the case takes on a new dimension. David Soul and Patrick Baladi guest star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: It's Murray and Bailey this week, as Rachel travels to Bristol with the DCI, who is advising on a rape and murder case that bears similarities to one she worked on 13 years previously involving the deaths of four women. Back at home, meanwhile, the detective discovers her brother Dom has been prostituting himself in her flat - and also learns the charges against her ex-boyfriend Nick are being dropped

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is asked to investigate the theft of a car belonging to a friend of Mullett, but misjudges the grizzled Superintendent's capacity for tact and diplomacy and it is not long before he is taken off the case. Things take a shocking turn when the car finally turns up - with the body of a drug dealer in the boot. Detective drama, starring David Jason, with Melanie Thaw and Bruce Alexander

John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Simon Callow, Roberta Taylor and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The irascible medic and Louisa embark on what seems to be a perfect first date at a classical music performance in the grounds of a country house, but an inappropriate remark from the doctor kills off any chance of romance. programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: News of Martin and Louisa's engagement spreads rapidly through the village, and Aunt Joan is annoyed to hear about it from Dave rather than her nephew. Al steps in to help as Bert struggles to keep the restaurant afloat, while the doctor is puzzled when he learns Beth's knee replacement wound is failing to respond to the medication he prescribed. Comedy drama series, starring Martin Clunes

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The duo look into the murder of an eight-year-old boy, coming up against a paedophile who cruelly deceives his victim's family. Dom's HIV test comes back negative, so he sets out to celebrate - only to endanger his own life and jeopardise Rachel's sergeant's exam in the process. Janet tells Andy she is having second thoughts about their brief liaison, but he finds her decision hard to accept. Suranne Jones and Lesley Sharp star, with Liam Boyle, Nicholas Gleaves and Joe Duttine

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour detective Jack Frost investigates a woman's near-fatal shooting. The victim was all set to testify against a notorious drugs baron accused of murdering one of his employees, but the appearance of a contract killer makes her think again - and it appears someone on Jack's team is leaking information. Meanwhile, the station is turned upside down in the hunt for a missing lottery ticket. David Jason, Michael McKell and Bruce Alexander star

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Mr Roper plans to convert the flats into smaller dwelling units - but he has not bargained for Robin, Chrissy and Jo having a three-year lease. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Simon corners Sam and assures him that what he witnessed was just a prank and nobody got hurt. Alya is furious to discover that Geoff left everything to Tim, who tells Adam that he doesn't want a penny of Geoff's money. Kai accuses Johnny of dealing drugs, and Nina tells Asha she's going bat watching with Roy

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate the deaths of a female pensioner and a vicar who were both killed during Broughton village's annual contest between men and women. The pair soon discover the preacher had a secret that could provide a motive - but when another body is found, they realise the answer to all the deaths lies in the history books. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the popular crime drama, starring John Thaw as author Colin Dexter's Oxford detective who treats his cases as intellectual challenges. In this mystery, Morse is introduced to trusty Sergeant Lewis (Kevin Whately) and takes it upon himself to investigate the apparent suicide of a lady friend. Co-starring Gemma Jones, Patrick Troughton, James Grout and Peter Woodthorpe

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: When a member of Oxford University's examinations board is found dead, Morse deduces he must have overheard a conversation someone wanted to keep secret at all costs. However, his theory has one major flaw - the victim was deaf. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Barbara Flynn, Michael Gough, Clive Swift, Roger Lloyd Pack and Frederick Treves

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: A royal visit to a family owned munitions factory puts a spring in the step of Chief Superintendent Bright, but Morse is still stuck on general duties and excluded from the event. However, when an unpopular worker is found murdered in a secluded area of the shop floor, the detective finds himself drawn into the affairs of the company and its dysfunctional owners. The prime suspect is a colleague who had fallen out with the victim over an industrial accident, and Bright is eager for the investigation to be carried out as quickly and quietly as possible, but Endeavour is not convinced the case can be solved so easily. Shaun Evans stars, with Martin Jarvis, Jenny Seagrove and Craig Parkinson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The headmaster of a local school is murdered, his body found pinned to the wall in the manner of his own butterfly collection. The dead man was a member of a mysterious international club for geniuses, which are currently testing the school's pupils for possible new recruits. Barnaby and Winter investigate the secretive organisation, which has proven controversial in the local area, but also uncover the possibility of a connection between the killing and the impact of pesticides on a rare species of butterfly

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel has a lucky escape while walking home when she narrowly avoids being hit by a car. Shaken, she starts pursuing the driver, who has fled on foot, and the next day she suspects the incident was an attempted murder - and that Nick may be involved. As she tries to deal with the revelation, Janet is left to take charge of the latest case - the gang shooting of a teenager whose friend is a key witness. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Part one of two. The badly burned body of a disabled man turns up in a remote part of Manchester and a few days later another corpse is discovered. The detectives start to realise the victims were not just murdered, but tortured too. They make an arrest, but it becomes clear their investigation has just scratched the surface. Meanwhile, Janet throws her husband out and Rachel's estranged brother turns up on her doorstep. With Lisa Riley and Pippa Haywood

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid agrees to buy Mike whatever he wants for his birthday - but is shocked by his request. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre is amazed when Jon gives her a gold credit card. Les is furious to see Leanne has a bruised face, and Fred discovers the source of the mysterious smell

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tricia regrets her night of passion with Terry, Alice returns to Emmerdale, Bernice is intrigued when she receives some flowers, and the strain gets too much for Kathy

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Betty worries about Kathy's state of mind, Terry ruins Kelly's 18th birthday party, and Eric asks Ashley to hold a toy antiques fair at the village hall

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick and Kate investigate possible arson attacks on barns in the area, while a wandering horse and a stray salmon add to the problems in Aidensfield. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A death in the family causes problems for Sid. Comedy, starring Diana Coupland

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin regrets sampling Mr Roper's home brew when he is breathalysed during a driving lesson with Chrissy. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A philandering husband is found dying by the swimming pool after being shot during a house party at the home of Lady Lucy Angkatell. Legendary Belgian Sleuth Poirot, who is guest of honour at the gathering, appears to have an open-and-shut case on his hands - but the investigator finds himself up against one of his finest adversaries. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A feud between warring neighbours turns into a siege when Nick is held at gunpoint. Meanwhile, Kate puts her own life at risk to save a dying man. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The village is buzzing with the news of Terry's outburst. Rachel asks Dr Khan's advice about Kathy, and Lyn is fed up with Marlon not paying her any attention

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Woolpack finally reopens, Chris and Zoe discover that Tara plans to sell Home Farm, and Butch threatens Paddy when he sees him talking to Mandy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack presses Alec for information about his dating agency. Maud admits to Ashley that she planted the fish in the shop, and Judy is horrified when Zoe visits her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Judy refuses to talk to Zoe and panics. Leanne encourages Zoe to fight for her rights, and Fred tells Ashley that he wants him to manage the shop

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective investigates a murder in the vicinity of a quiet country church, whose previous vicar committed suicide. As the case proceeds, he uncovers a sinister nest of evil-doing. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Michael Hordern, Angela Morant and Maurice O'Connell

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth finds himself in interesting company as English socialites and American millionaires rub shoulders on board a train bound for the French Riviera. Passengers include oil magnate Rufus Van Aldin (Elliott Gould) and his daughter Ruth (Jaime Murray) - to whom he has given a magnificent ruby known as the Heart of Fire. With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A visiting American academic delivers a controversial speech, with many audience members worried his ideas could be used to target ethnic minorities. So when he is found hanged the next morning and Dr Hobson quickly discounts suicide, Lewis and Hathaway realise they have many suspects - from the local anti-racism activist who was first in voicing her disgust at the professor's beliefs to the women who had bombarded him with threatening e-mails. But then another body turns up and the case takes on a new dimension. David Soul and Patrick Baladi guest star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: It's Murray and Bailey this week, as Rachel travels to Bristol with the DCI, who is advising on a rape and murder case that bears similarities to one she worked on 13 years previously involving the deaths of four women. Back at home, meanwhile, the detective discovers her brother Dom has been prostituting himself in her flat - and also learns the charges against her ex-boyfriend Nick are being dropped

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is asked to investigate the theft of a car belonging to a friend of Mullett, but misjudges the grizzled Superintendent's capacity for tact and diplomacy and it is not long before he is taken off the case. Things take a shocking turn when the car finally turns up - with the body of a drug dealer in the boot. Detective drama, starring David Jason, with Melanie Thaw and Bruce Alexander

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris discovers that Home Farm will be sold at auction. Butch catches Paddy and Mandy together on her bed, and Kathy breaks down in front of Biff

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy gets upset when Dr Khan asks her about her mental state. Viv and Vic fall out over Andy, Billy Hopwood returns to Emmerdale, and Chris convinces Kathy to see a counsellor

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary warns Zoe to stay away from them, Deirdre and Jon move into their new home, Des warns Les to stay away from Samantha, and Leanne is furious when Nick snubs her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des finds his car windows smashed in and believes Les did it. Fred is horrified to learn Ashley has employed Maud, and Judy asks Des and Samantha to be Katie's godparents

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An American tourist's body is discovered in her hotel room, apparently dead from a heart attack - but a valuable Anglo-Saxon jewel she intended to donate to the Ashmolean Museum is missing and several interested parties are acting strangely. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Simon Callow, Roberta Taylor and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The irascible medic and Louisa embark on what seems to be a perfect first date at a classical music performance in the grounds of a country house, but an inappropriate remark from the doctor kills off any chance of romance. Elsewhere, Al worries that Pauline's gambling is getting out of hand when he finds out that her moped has been repossessed. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: Part two of two. The team come to the aid of a party of holidaymakers on an Austrian mountain _ who are unaware they are being held hostage

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A racehorse trainer asks Holmes to investigate the mysterious disappearance of one of his cash-strapped employer's many creditors. Victorian whodunit, starring Jeremy Brett and Edward Hardwicke, with a young Jude Law in an early supporting role

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar investigates the murder of a journalist friend, following a trail of clues that leads to a cult obsessed with the splendour and savagery of ancient Rome. Drama, guest starring Peter Wyngarde and Ed Bishop

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A member of a group of convicts slips off the roof and dies during a robbery. The apparent leader of the gang appears to be having doubts about his life and Regan senses he is deliberately pointing clues in a desire to be caught during his next job. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed helps Scotland Yard colleague Arthur Doyle on a murder inquiry with an abundance of clues, each one pointing to an illustrious suspect. However, all the clues prove fakes left behind by a blackmailer who is planting them to incriminate wealthy men as part of an extortion racket. Kenneth Cope guest stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur finds it increasingly hard to sell his dodgy second-hand cars and decides it might be more profitable to rent them out instead. However, panic ensues when his beloved Bentley is stolen. Meanwhile, Ray faces the possibility that his new girlfriend is involved with a gang of burglars. Drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: How traditional seaside rock is made, a process that has not changed since Victorian times. Plus, how a new fire extinguisher is saving lives around the world. Jimmy Nail narrates

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A desperate girl contacts Holmes and asks him to clear her boyfriend of killing his father, a farmer found bludgeoned to death beside a lake. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett , Peter Vaughan and James Purefoy

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar is suspected of involvement in a series of jewel robberies on the French Riviera, and has to stay one step ahead of the police long enough to catch the real culprit. Guest starring Ronnie Barker and Aimee Delamain

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A bad dream has Regan's girlfriend fearing for his safety, and her visions ring true when he gets involved in a case involving a stolen lorryload of cigars. The plot thickens when illicit cocaine is retrieved and arms stolen from the US are discovered to be on their way to a military faction in Ireland. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Tara King finds herself the subject of an all too welcoming holiday resort with a 'no check-out' policy for some of their guests in this comedic parody of Patrick McGoohan's iconic series The Prisoner. Linda Thorson stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Australian private investigator Bill McCabe approaches Arthur and tells him he is the possible heir of Joshua Daley, who died intestate in Woolomoroo. Believing kinship has been proved, the dodgy dealer prepares to fly Down Under to claim what appears to be rightfully his. George Cole stars

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: Jimmy Nail narrates a look inside a firework factory, before a lesson in pork pies in Melton Mowbray. Plus, how respective experts create fishing rods and single malt whisky

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five called Young's Offenders answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge. Then they try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Heptathlete Louise Hazel, comedian Joel Dommett, 1980s music star Sinitta and actor Les Dennis team up to take part in the quiz show, in which they must answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit - all without being caught out by the 'quiz genius' Chaser. Presented by Bradley Walsh

programme.title: Taken 2 (2012)
programme.desc: Former CIA agent Bryan Mills and his ex-wife Lenore are taken hostage in Istanbul by an Albanian gangster, whose son Bryan killed while bringing down a kidnapping ring. He is able to escape with the aid of his daughter, and together they fight to rescue Lenore and wipe out the criminal family. Action thriller sequel, starring Liam Neeson, Maggie Grace and Famke Janssen

programme.desc: The latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Ransom (1996)
programme.desc: An airline tycoon is wary of involving the police when his nine-year-old son is abducted, but co-operates with their plan to make a ransom drop. However, when the trade-off goes badly wrong, he decides to take matters into his own hands by placing a $2million bounty on the kidnappers' heads, to the consternation of the cops and his anxious wife. Thriller, starring Mel Gibson, Rene Russo, Gary Sinise, Delroy Lindo and Liev Schreiber

programme.title: Live Darts: UK Open
programme.desc: Jacqui Oatley and Ned Boulting present coverage of the evening session on day two from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring matches from the sixth round. programme.title: The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey (2012)
programme.desc: Unassuming hobbit Bilbo Baggins is pulled out of his comfortable life when Gandalf the wizard volunteers him for a dangerous quest. He joins a party of dwarves who are on a mission to reclaim their lost homeland, which has been taken over by a dragon. When the group journeys into the Misty Mountains, Bilbo becomes separated from the dwarves and falls into a cave, where he finds himself confronted by Gollum and forced to play a riddle game. It is set in Middle-earth 60 years before the events of The Lord of the Rings and is the first part of Peter Jackson's fantasy adventure trilogy based on JRR Tolkien's novel, starring Martin Freeman, Ian McKellen, Richard Armitage, James Nesbitt, Ken Stott, Cate Blanchett, Ian Holm and Christopher Lee

Comedy adventure, with Tom Cruise, Cameron Diaz and Viola Davis

With champagne-popping nights out in party capital Mykonos, and the stunning sunsets on the island of Santorini, this cruise offers the perfect combination of ancient Greek history and exciting modern landscapes

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper's New Victims
programme.desc: Peter Sutcliffe, Britain's notorious serial killer, was convicted of 13 murders and seven attempted murders. Last November, he died in prison. But what dark secrets did he take to the grave? This documentary claims to draw on new evidence and testimony to reveal more victims before the official count, revealing that, contrary to the original police investigation, his attacks were carried out on men as well as women

programme.title: Countdown to Murder
programme.desc: The investigation into the death of Penny Beale in November 2001. Her boyfriend Michael Moffat claimed she fell down the stairs while drunk - but her mother knew better. To the police, Michael presented as a charming, helpful man who had been struggling to save his girlfriend from her alcohol and drug addictions. Penny's mother was aware of another side of him however - he was a drunk, abusive and violent man who was now trying to get away with her daughter's murder

programme.title: Fritzl: Surviving Evil
programme.desc: The case of the Austrian man Josef Fritzl, who kept his daughter prisoner for 24 years in the cellar of his house, until she was released in 2008 at the age of 42. Dramatically pleading guilty to all of the charges at the last moment of the trial, he was sentenced to life in a secure unit for incest, imprisonment, raping the victim - who bore seven children - and causing the death of one child by neglect

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: The singer begins her Portuguese adventure in Porto, where she discovers the roots of a similarly named fortified wine. She then boards the impressive vessel that will take her down the River Douro. The cruise delivers her to the architectural wonder of Lamego, Vila Real and the home of Mateus Rose. She even manages to hop over the border to Spain to visit the city of Salamanca. There is plenty of food, drink and local culture on offer on Jane's luxurious cruise, which ends its journey in the capital city of Lisbon

programme.title: The Gadget Show
programme.desc: Jon Bentley sees how three budget 5G smartphones fare on a whistle-stop tour of Liverpool. Along with Ortis Deley, Jon also looks over some astronomy-related tech. Georgie Barrat assesses the best headphones available for under £50, while Harry Wallop looks an app that promises to not only help people save on the food bill, but also prevent waste. Craig Charles offers a rundown of the hottest deals from the tech world and a brand-new competition that is anything but budget

programme.title: Kate Humble's Coastal Britain
programme.desc: Starting in Dorset on Stonebarrow Hill, the naturalist walks a 13-mile stretch of the South West Coast Path. Her first stop is at Charmouth, a place well-known for fossil hunting, where local girl Mary Anning found an ichthyosaur in the early 19th century. At Lyme Regis, Kate discovers the lengths and costs engineers have gone to in order to protect the town, before visiting a local mill listed in the Doomsday Book. After a spell bird spotting in the Seaton Wetlands, Kate enjoys a tour of the area's limestone mines before making her way to Beer Point, which boasts wonderful views of the sea

programme.title: Gregg Wallace: Big Weekends Away
programme.desc: The host enjoys a three-day trip in York. While it's a small city, York packs in almost 2,000 years of history and has been at the centre of some of the most important events in British history. Heading to the famous Gothic York Minster, climbing the city walls and taking a tour that focuses on the life of Guy Fawkes, Gregg uncovers a history that introduces him to Romans, Vikings, and medieval and Tudor kings. He also meets one of Yorkshire's best chefs, who cooks a menu filled with one of Gregg's favourite ingredients - rhubarb

programme.title: Britain's Favourite 80s Songs
programme.desc: Toyah Willcox, Paul Gambaccini, Cheryl Baker and Clare Grogan are among those recalling the music and memories of 1981. It was a year when Bucks Fizz were losing their skirts, Adam and the Ants were re-inventing the pop video, and synthesizer bands were making it big. However, there was still room in the charts for the retro sound of Shakin' Stevens. Narrated by Tony Blackburn

programme.title: That's So
programme.desc: An entertaining reflection on the year, which saw the wedding of Lady Diana Spencer and Prince Charles draw a global record audience of 750 million people, US president Ronald Reagan and Pope John Paul II survive assassination attempts, the first Rubik's Cube world championship, the Magic Stick become the latest must-have toy and in music the emergence of the New Romantic era

programme.desc: The comedian explores Devon and Cornwall in her vintage camper van, taking in some dramatic scenery, unspoilt countryside and incredible historic sights along the way. She stumbles across one of the area's more unique attractions, including The Gnome Reserve, where she meets a local who has a collection of more than 1,000 gnomes, and finds out where the term 'stickler' comes from by taking part in the ancient sport of Cornish Wrestling, before ending her journey at the stunning coastal viewpoint of St Bedruthan Steps

programme.title: Inside the World's Greatest Hotels
programme.desc: A look behind the scenes at the Dorchester in London's affluent area of Mayfair, which has been associated with the rich and famous since it first opened in 1931.
