programme.title: Nils Holgersson
programme.desc: Gorgo and Akka isolated far from the flock and witness an aurora borealis

programme.title: Nils Holgersson
programme.desc: The flock is trying to have a well-deserved rest when they are kept awake by Reggie the partying bat

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Kong: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Animated fantasy adventures

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: The boys build a submarine to look for treasure underwater, but run into a band of pirates and a hungry sea dragon

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: Jack and Tashi trick their way on to a ghost train after getting turned away for being too small, only to find the ride has a sinister secret

programme.title: Pocket Dragon Adventures
programme.desc: A group of very small dragons live with a kindly old wizard, they're not big enough to fly but that doesn't stop them having many adventures

programme.title: Once a Soldier
programme.desc: Open for over 300 years and home to 320 old soldiers, the Royal Hospital in Chelsea is under threat. This series follows the residents for a year of their lives

programme.title: The Queen's Cavalry
programme.desc: The armoured divisions visit Canada for a mock battle, in which conditions are exactly as expected during a war - except lasers are used instead of live ammunition. Back in London, the trainee troopers don their breastplates as the ceremonial season begins, and prepare to guard the Queen for the first time

programme.title: Danger Man
programme.desc: John Drake (Patrick McGoohan), a secret agent working for NATO, travels to different corners of the world in order to prevent crimes that threaten world peace

programme.title: The Old Guys
programme.desc: Comedy series, starring Roger Lloyd Pack and Clive Swift, about two lively pensioners who see their age as no barrier to having fun - particularly when it comes to their attractive neighbour, played by Jane Asher. Written by Sam Bain and Jesse Armstrong, creators of Channel 4's hit sitcom Peep Show, and Simon Blackwell

programme.title: St Paul's
programme.desc: Part two. The Dean and Chapter are forced to consider increasing admission cost to help maintain the building

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: The extraordinary stories of some of the British men and women who fought or lived through the Second World War, now in their nineties or older. The first episode looks at how national unity was tested in the 1920s and 30s with class conflict, soaring unemployment and widespread civil unrest, but despite these upheavals, those growing up in the inter-war years remember much that was positive about life in Britain at the time. When war broke out in 1939, the men and women of this generation were ready to risk their lives for their country, but the evacuation of the British Army from the beaches of Dunkirk was a wake-up call for those expecting a swift victory

programme.title: The Maggie (1954)
programme.desc: A brash American businessman hires a small Scottish company to move a set of valuable furniture to his new home on a remote island. However, he soon learns that his belongings are to be transported on a dilapidated boat in desperate need of repair - and sets out in hot pursuit of them. Ealing comedy, with Paul Douglas, Alex Mackenzie, James Copeland and Tommy Kearins

programme.title: Doctor at Large (1957)
programme.desc: Newly qualified doctor Simon Sparrow leaves St Swithin's Hospital and relocates to the countryside in search of a job at two prospering practices. However, before long he runs into fearsome former mentor Sir Lancelot Spratt. Comedy, starring Dirk Bogarde, James Robertson Justice and Donald Sinden

programme.title: Seven Thunders (1957)
programme.desc: Two British soldiers escape from German prison camps and hide out in a fishing village in occupied France. As they wait for the opportunity to return to England, they attract the attention of a doctor who is also a serial killer preying on fugitives from the Nazis. Second World War drama based on Rupert Croft-Cooke's novel, starring Stephen Boyd and James Robertson Justice

programme.title: Discover London
programme.desc: Explore hidden gems and alternative areas of the capital, learning more about the cultural history of one of the world's greatest cities

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Peter Maneas embarks on a journey through some of the most spectacular and unique Greek islands, exploring the rich culture, history and food of Greece while meeting the locals and those who live there

programme.title: The Old Guys
programme.desc: Comedy series, starring Roger Lloyd Pack and Clive Swift, about two lively pensioners who see their age as no barrier to having fun - particularly when it comes to their attractive neighbour, played by Jane Asher. Written by Sam Bain and Jesse Armstrong, creators of Channel 4's hit sitcom Peep Show, and Simon Blackwell

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: The Escort (2015)
programme.desc: A sex-obsessed journalist whose career is falling apart has a chance encounter in a bar with a high-class prostitute. Seeing a chance to write the story that will make him a success, he persuades her to let him document her life, and a strange connection develops between the two lonely outsiders. Comedy drama, starring Michael Doneger, Lyndsy Fonseca and Bruce Campbell

programme.title: Kidnapping Freddy Heineken (2015)
programme.desc: Five men struggling to make ends meet decide to kidnap a beer tycoon who is one of Europe's richest men. The plan is a success, and they demand the biggest ransom in history - but tensions within the group threaten their chances of escaping the law. Fact-based crime drama, starring Anthony Hopkins, Jim Sturgess and Sam Worthington

programme.title: Jailhouse Wardress (1981)
programme.desc: A female Nazi prison warden goes into hiding in South America after the Second World War. She oversees a secret facility where genetically engineered sex slaves are created for her fellow war criminals, while also plotting revenge for the Third Reich's defeat. Drama, starring Monica Swinn and Michael Bates

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing Notting Hill for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Swan Corner
programme.desc: A day in the life on the High Street in West Wickham, shot in 1968 on 8mm film. Showing activities at different times of the day, including The Swan Pub

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: The extraordinary stories of some of the British men and women who fought or lived through the Second World War, now in their nineties or older. The first episode looks at how national unity was tested in the 1920s and 30s with class conflict, soaring unemployment and widespread civil unrest, but despite these upheavals, those growing up in the inter-war years remember much that was positive about life in Britain at the time. When war broke out in 1939, the men and women of this generation were ready to risk their lives for their country, but the evacuation of the British Army from the beaches of Dunkirk was a wake-up call for those expecting a swift victory

programme.title: Once a Soldier
programme.desc: Open for over 300 years and home to 320 old soldiers, the Royal Hospital in Chelsea is under threat. This series follows the residents for a year of their lives

programme.title: The Queen's Cavalry
programme.desc: The armoured divisions visit Canada for a mock battle, in which conditions are exactly as expected during a war - except lasers are used instead of live ammunition. Back in London, the trainee troopers don their breastplates as the ceremonial season begins, and prepare to guard the Queen for the first time

programme.title: Pocket Dragon Adventures
programme.desc: A group of very small dragons live with a kindly old wizard, they're not big enough to fly but that doesn't stop them having many adventures

programme.title: Nils Holgersson
programme.desc: The flock lands on an island paradise, where Soft Feather and her sisters talk about nothing but handsome ganders

programme.title: Nils Holgersson
programme.desc: The wild geese are flying quickly, fleeing a thunderstorm, when Nils spots a small forest, ideal for rest

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Kong: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Animated fantasy adventures

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: Jack stows away on a flying pirate ship to get back his stolen video games console

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: Jack and Tashi awaken mummies from a tomb, and the Baron manages to control them with a magic amulet

programme.title: Pocket Dragon Adventures
programme.desc: A group of very small dragons live with a kindly old wizard, they're not big enough to fly but that doesn't stop them having many adventures

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: The extraordinary stories of some of the British men and women who fought or lived through the Second World War, now in their nineties or older. The first episode looks at how national unity was tested in the 1920s and 30s with class conflict, soaring unemployment and widespread civil unrest, but despite these upheavals, those growing up in the inter-war years remember much that was positive about life in Britain at the time. When war broke out in 1939, the men and women of this generation were ready to risk their lives for their country, but the evacuation of the British Army from the beaches of Dunkirk was a wake-up call for those expecting a swift victory

programme.title: Untamed Romania
programme.desc: In Romania, ancient forests and expansive wetlands provide habitats to some of Europe's most iconic creatures. This is a celebration of nature, in all its beauty and diversity

programme.title: The Old Guys
programme.desc: Comedy series, starring Roger Lloyd Pack and Clive Swift, about two lively pensioners who see their age as no barrier to having fun - particularly when it comes to their attractive neighbour, played by Jane Asher. Written by Sam Bain and Jesse Armstrong, creators of Channel 4's hit sitcom Peep Show, and Simon Blackwell

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dermot is sacked for dangling a customer by the ankles, while Gary, on the other hand, is offered a new post. However, he has difficulty breaking the news to George and Anthea. Laddish comedy, starring Harry Enfield and Martin Clunes

programme.title: The Slipper and the Rose (1976)
programme.desc: A girl is reduced to being the downtrodden servant of her cruel stepmother after her father's death. With a little help from her fairy godmother, she wins the heart of a handsome prince, who is desperately trying to avoid being forced into a political marriage by his parents. Musical version of Cinderella, starring Gemma Craven and Richard Chamberlain

programme.title: Assassin for Hire (1951)
programme.desc: An assassin who kills to finance his violinist brother's career is tricked into confessing his crimes by a wily police inspector. Drama, starring Sydney Tafler, Ronald Howard, Katherine Blake and John Hewer

programme.title: Portrait from Life (1949)
programme.desc: A British army officer searches for a Jewish refugee in post-war Germany, only to discover that she has lost her memory. Romantic mystery, starring Robert Beatty and Mai Zetterling

programme.title: The Old Guys
programme.desc: Comedy series, starring Roger Lloyd Pack and Clive Swift, about two lively pensioners who see their age as no barrier to having fun - particularly when it comes to their attractive neighbour, played by Jane Asher. Written by Sam Bain and Jesse Armstrong, creators of Channel 4's hit sitcom Peep Show, and Simon Blackwell

programme.title: Best of The Tube
programme.desc: Recapping the finest moments from the music show that was hosted by Jools Holland and the late Paula Yates

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Sail the Saronic and Ionian Greek islands with Peter Maneas as he explores their rich history, culture, cuisine, and people as well as their hidden secrets and surprises

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary goes to the pub to drown his sorrows, so Dermot invites Deborah to their flat for a romantic evening of candlelight, dinner and music. Laddish comedy, starring Harry Enfield and Martin Clunes

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dermot is sacked for dangling a customer by the ankles, while Gary, on the other hand, is offered a new post. However, he has difficulty breaking the news to George and Anthea. Laddish comedy, starring Harry Enfield and Martin Clunes

programme.title: Purge of Kingdoms (2019)
programme.desc: The leaders of eight rival noble houses gather for a festival designed to settle their long-standing feuds once and for all. Meanwhile, the king who rules over them all has to contend with his own family plotting to have him assassinated. Fantasy spoof, starring Angus MacFadyen and Lou Ferrigno

programme.title: Doorways (2015)
programme.desc: An insecure student takes a job as a bouncer to prove his masculinity, and is taken under the wing of a hardened veteran of the job. As he learns the tricks of the trade, he is drawn on a dangerous journey with murderous consequences that will teach him there is more than one way to be a real man. Drama, starring Steven Murphy and Jonathan Ciardini

programme.title: Felicity (1978)
programme.desc: A teenager in a remote Catholic girls' school seeks solace from her repressive life in reading erotic novels and having a secret love affair with one of her classmates. A trip to Hong Kong organised by her parents opens her eyes to more experiences - both good and bad. Australian drama, starring Glory Annen and Jody Hanson

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: A distressed teenager explores cures for hair-loss, and a professional singer tries to save her career by learning to control her severe acid reflux. A mother fears her six-year-old son will be a target for bullying because of his psoriasis, and doctors help an obese father reverse damage done to his liver

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Maria Marten: Or, Murder in the Red Barn (1935)
programme.desc: An evil squire lures a woman into marriage, but when she becomes pregnant, he decides to murder her, leaving a young gypsy who is in love with her to take the blame. Period drama, starring Tod Slaughter and Sophie Stewart

programme.title: Snow White
programme.desc: Production by the London Children's Ballet. Snow White finds herself in the company of seven trusty dwarfs after her evil stepmother attempts her revenge on her

programme.title: Fairy Tales for You
programme.desc: An animated version of Goldilocks and the Three Bears read by Rik Mayall

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: Exploits at West Poley (1985)
programme.desc: Two boys exploring an underground cave divert the course of a river, with surprising repercussions for two towns. Children's period drama, with Brenda Fricker and Charlie Condou

programme.title: One Good Turn (1954)
programme.desc: A boy living in an orphanage grows up to be the institution's lovable but clumsy caretaker. He must save the children's home from a wicked property developer who wants to build a factory on the site, and tricks him into taking the kids to the seaside so he can knock the building down. Comedy, starring Norman Wisdom and Joan Rice

programme.title: The Likely Lads (1976)
programme.desc: Bob and Thelma embark on a chaotic caravan holiday - with best pal Terry and a glamorous Scandinavian au pair in tow, ensuring that nothing will go according to plan. Comedy based on the TV series, starring James Bolam, Rodney Bewes, Brigit Forsyth and Mary Tamm

programme.title: Seven Thunders (1957)
programme.desc: Two British soldiers escape from German prison camps and hide out in a fishing village in occupied France. As they wait for the opportunity to return to England, they attract the attention of a doctor who is also a serial killer preying on fugitives from the Nazis. Second World War drama based on Rupert Croft-Cooke's novel, starring Stephen Boyd and James Robertson Justice

programme.title: The Maggie (1954)
programme.desc: A brash American businessman hires a small Scottish company to move a set of valuable furniture to his new home on a remote island. However, he soon learns that his belongings are to be transported on a dilapidated boat in desperate need of repair - and sets out in hot pursuit of them. Ealing comedy, with Paul Douglas, Alex Mackenzie, James Copeland and Tommy Kearins

programme.title: Swan Corner
programme.desc: A day in the life on the High Street in West Wickham, shot in 1968 on 8mm film. Showing activities at different times of the day, including The Swan Pub

programme.title: All You Need Is Love
programme.desc: The origins of R'n'B and soul, with contributions by Aretha Franklin, Ike and Tina Turner, Wilson Pickett, Stevie Wonder, Bo Diddley and the Supremes

programme.title: A Random Encounter (2013)
programme.desc: Two strangers trying to make their way in Hollywood meet by chance in a coffee shop, and come away unable to stop thinking about one another. However, they both soon discover that they have no way of tracking the other down. Romantic comedy, starring Abby Wathen, Michael Rady, Meghan Markle and Sean Young

programme.title: The Comic Strip Presents
programme.desc: Part one of two. A security guard plans a bank robbery to finance a rescue mission when his long-distance girlfriend gets caught up in an invasion of the Falkland Islands

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Joe returns to the street after having a lobotomy, while Mrs Hugett comes up with a plan to get money out of Jack. Comedy, written and performed by John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: Kidnapping Freddy Heineken (2015)
programme.desc: Five men struggling to make ends meet decide to kidnap a beer tycoon who is one of Europe's richest men. Fact-based crime drama, starring Anthony Hopkins, Jim Sturgess and Sam Worthington

programme.title: The Queen's Cavalry
programme.desc: Insight into the daily lives of the troopers of the Household Cavalry, the British Army's most senior regiments. programme.title: The Queen's Cavalry
programme.desc: The Princess Elizabeth Cup sees troopers compete to be the smartest in the Household Cavalry. Recruits seeking promotion attend gruelling tests involving live rounds and grenades, and the trainee horse-riders attempt their first canter

programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner

programme.title: Football Years
programme.desc: A look back at the 1991-92 season, in which Manchester United and Leeds United fought for the title in the final campaign before the arrival of the Premiership. The Red Devils were favourites to end their long title drought as Liverpool faltered with an ageing side, but Howard Wilkinson's men refused to give up the chase

programme.title: EFL Highlights
programme.desc: Crystal Palace v Watford. Action from the 2012/13 Championship play-off final at Wembley Stadium, as the teams contested a place in the top flight alongside the already promoted Cardiff City and Hull City. Palace reached this match when Wilfried Zaha scored a brace in a 2-0 away win against Brighton & Hove Albion after a goalless draw in the first leg. Meanwhile, Watford bounced back from narrowly missing out on automatic promotion by overcoming Leicester City in dramatic fashion, with Troy Deeney scoring an injury time-winner on the counter after Manuel Almunia had saved a penalty
